title: Research: casting
description: Design the casting node and produce its framework per AiModel-645.9. Classification: vibe-neutral to borderline vibe-critical. This node sits after ad-likeness-lint and before shot-compiler. It selects the virtual model (appearance, age, personality feel), wardrobe, environment, lighting, and props — the visual identity that shot-compiler and render-spec-compiler will reference in every scene. In the Cocoon experiment, this was handled as continuity_tokens inside beat-planner-shot-compiler. In the new pipeline, casting is its own step because: (1) story/beats/mood nodes decide WHAT happens, (2) casting decides WHO appears and WHERE, (3) shot-compiler decides HOW it looks. The cast identity must be consistent across all generated video clips. Reference: continuity_tokens in test_prompts/beat_planner_shot_prompt_complier.json.
close_reason: Framework written to docs/plans/2026-04-15-casting-framework.md. Matches story character to persona library, adapts wardrobe/environment, falls back to human gate when no match. Depends on persona library being queryable (see new bead for persona library infrastructure).

title: Epic 3: Validation And Preview
description: Build the graph validator (cycle detection, missing inputs, incompatible types, orphan/disabled nodes), preview engine with incremental recompute and 150ms debounce, data inspector panel with payload viewer/schema diff/lineage view, inspector validation and preview tabs, and edge visual states with validation badges. Key files: src/features/workflows/domain/graph-validator.ts, src/features/workflows/domain/preview-engine.ts, src/features/data-inspector/, src/features/inspector/. Success criteria: Invalid graphs explain themselves, previews update on config and connection changes.

title: Graph validator
description: Implement GraphValidator per plan sections 16.1-16.3. Workflow-level: unique node/edge ids, existing source/target nodes, ports exist on templates, acyclic graph (cycle detection), disabled node warnings. Node-level: config schema validation, required inputs connected, unsupported mixed modes, review node config. Edge-level: source=output/target=input, compatibility check, no duplicate incompatible edges to single-value ports, no self-loops. multiple:true inputs ordered by targetOrder. File: src/features/workflows/domain/graph-validator.ts. Acceptance: Returns ValidationIssue[] with correct codes, severity, suggestions. Unit tests for each rule.

title: Preview engine
description: Implement PreviewEngine per plan section 10. Deterministic preview derivation from config + upstream previews. Incremental recompute: on node change recompute that node, invalidate downstream caches, recompute downstream in topological order, stop at invalid nodes. Performance: topology-change immediate, text-entry config debounce 150ms, expensive formatting memoized by input/config hash. Fixture-aware: previews use selected fixture when available. Preview is synchronous/near-synchronous, distinct from mock execution. File: src/features/workflows/domain/preview-engine.ts. Acceptance: Previews update within 150ms of config change, deterministic for same config+inputs+fixture, memoized correctly.

title: Data inspector panel
description: Build the Data Inspector as a first-class feature per plan section 6.5. Modes: node selected (show inputs/outputs), edge selected (show EdgePayloadSnapshot with source/transported/coercion), workflow selected (run summary), nothing selected (instructions). Payload viewer: status, schemaType, producer node, timestamp, previewText/previewUrl, size estimate, validation state. Inspector precedence: default to last successful run output when present, fall back to preview output when no run exists, label source as 'preview' or 'lastRun', allow side-by-side comparison. Schema diff view: expected vs actual schema comparison. Lineage view: trace payload origin through graph. Large-payload policy: inline up to 256KB, collapse/virtualize 256KB-2MB, download above 2MB. Raw JSON and readable summary modes. Files: src/features/data-inspector/components/data-inspector-panel.tsx, payload-viewer.tsx, schema-diff-view.tsx, lineage-view.tsx. Acceptance: (1) Node selection shows input/output payloads with metadata, (2) Edge selection shows EdgePayloadSnapshot with coercion info, (3) Payloads labeled as preview or lastRun, (4) Side-by-side comparison toggleable, (5) Payload >256KB collapsed with expand button, (6) Payload >2MB shows download-only, (7) Lineage traces to source node correctly, (8) Copy/download JSON works for all payload sizes.

title: Inspector validation tab

Show: config schema issues (from Zod validation), missing required inputs (unconnected ports), type mismatch issues (incompatible connections), warnings (coercion, disabled upstream), suggested remediations (e.g. 'Connect a scriptWriter output to this input'). Group by severity (error/warning/info). Each issue links to the relevant node/edge/port. File: src/features/inspector/components/validation-tab.tsx. Acceptance: All ValidationIssue types render correctly, suggestions are actionable, clicking an issue navigates to the source.

title: Inspector preview tab

Preview vs last-run comparison (side-by-side when both exist). Label source as 'preview' or 'lastRun'. Default to last successful run output when present, fall back to preview when no run exists. Acceptance: Preview output displays correctly for all node types, fixture selector works, comparison view shows differences between preview and last run.

title: Edge visuals and badges

title: UI/UX Design System Implementation
description: Implement the complete AI Video Workflow Builder Visual Design System per plans/ui-ux/04-final-design-system.md. Covers CSS tokens, typography, node cards, typed edges, inspector, run toolbar, node library, empty/loading/error states, z-index layering, motion system, responsive layout, data-testid strategy, and accessibility. acceptance_criteria: All 13 child tasks completed. Full design system implemented with dark mode, all component states, accessibility, and test IDs per spec.

title: CSS Token System Setup
description: Set up the CSS variable token system in the app's global CSS. Implement ALL 17 core shadcn tokens with dark-mode hex values: --background (#0B0D12), --foreground (#E6EAF2), --card (#11141B), --card-foreground (#E6EAF2), --primary (#38BDF8), --primary-foreground (#06131C), --secondary (#1A2230), --secondary-foreground (#D7DDEA), --muted (#151A22), --muted-foreground (#8A93A5), --accent (#162433), --accent-foreground (#DCEBFA), --destructive (#F43F5E), --destructive-foreground (#FFF1F4), --border (#252B36), --input (#2B3442), --ring (#38BDF8). Add 3 extension tokens: --signal (#F59E0B) for active execution/traversal, --success (#22C55E) for passed/completed, --warning (#F59E0B) for caution/degraded. Add 7 node category accent tokens: Input=slate/neutral, Script=blue, Visuals=violet, Audio=teal, Video=amber, Utility=gray, Output=emerald. Category color appears only as subtle accent line, icon tint, or tiny badge edge. Map all tokens to Tailwind config. Define tokens in :root and .dark. Follow tone rules: category color never replaces selection color, selection stays obvious through ring and border, error overrides category accents, disabled/stale desaturate category accents, no large gradients or full-card semantic fills for normal states. Extend Tailwind only for signal, success, and warning. Prefer semantic utility classes over arbitrary color values. Reference: design system sections 4, 20.
acceptance_criteria: All core theme tokens defined in CSS variables. Extension tokens (signal, success, warning) available. Node category accent tokens defined. Tailwind config extended for custom tokens. Dark mode working. Category color tone rules followed.

title: Motion and Transition System
description: Implement timing specs per section 8. ALL 7 TIMINGS: (1) Hover transitions: 120ms ease-out, (2) Focus and selection transitions: 150ms ease-out, (3) Tab content transitions: 160ms ease-out, (4) Inspector data refresh pulse: 180ms ease-out, (5) Execution edge traversal: 900ms linear repeating while active, (6) Running status dot pulse: 1200ms ease-in-out repeating while active, (7) Skeleton shimmer: 1400ms linear repeating. MOTION PRINCIPLES: No scale animation on focus-visible states. Prefer opacity, border, and ring changes over size changes. Execution animation emphasizes path order, not spectacle. Success settle animations brief, fade back to resting UI. REDUCED MOTION (prefers-reduced-motion): Remove shimmer, traveling dots, animated edge dashes. Keep only brief opacity or color transitions at 80ms. Replace execution animation with static active highlighting and timestamp updates. Avoid auto-scrolling inspector to changed content. Reference: design system section 8.
acceptance_criteria: All transitions match spec timing. Hover 120ms, focus 150ms, tab 160ms, pulse 1200ms, shimmer 1400ms, execution trace 900ms. prefers-reduced-motion removes animations and uses 80ms fallbacks. No scale animations on focus-visible.

title: Responsive Layout and Panel Resize
description: Implement responsive behavior per section 6. Left panel: 280px default, 240px min, 360px max. Right panel: 400px default, 320px min, 480px max. Center canvas fills remainder. Panel widths user-resizable with persisted preferences. Resize handles: 10px hit area (visible divider 1px). Double-click resets to default width. Breakpoints: below 1280px side panels may collapse to icon rail, below 1024px only one side panel open by default, below 768px default to read-only graph viewing. Collapse rules: collapsing preserves previous width, inspector keeps active tab on reopen, toolbar fixed regardless of collapse, canvas panning/selection stable on panel open/close. Reference: design system section 6.
acceptance_criteria: Panels resize with 10px hit area. Double-click resets width. Collapse to icon rail below 1280px. One panel default below 1024px. Read-only below 768px. Width preferences persist. Canvas stable on panel toggle. data-testid='panel-resize-left' and 'panel-resize-right' present.

ALL 26 REQUIRED TEST IDS: node-card-{nodeId}, node-port-in-{nodeId}-{portKey}, node-port-out-{nodeId}-{portKey}, edge-{edgeId}, edge-label-{edgeId}, inspector, inspector-tab-{tabName}, inspector-tabs (TabsList), run-toolbar, run-btn-workflow, run-btn-node, run-btn-from-here, run-btn-cancel, run-status-chip, workflow-save-btn, workflow-export-btn, workflow-dirty-indicator, node-search-input, node-menu-btn-{nodeId}, quick-add-dialog, connect-dialog, panel-resize-left, panel-resize-right, canvas-empty-cta, validation-item-{issueCode}, toast-run-error. STATE ATTRIBUTES on relevant components: data-selected='true', data-running='true', data-invalid='true', data-stale='true', data-readonly='true', data-hovered='true', data-dragging='true', data-disabled='true', data-type='video|audio|data' (on edge pills). Reference: design system section 18 plus test IDs found in Tailwind examples in sections 10, 11, 12, 13.
design: Files: All component files from tasks 3-8
acceptance_criteria: Every interactive element has data-testid per naming convention. State attributes (data-selected, data-running, data-invalid, data-stale, data-readonly) present on relevant components. No duplicate IDs. All IDs use kebab-case with semantic naming.

title: Accessibility Pass
description: Implement a11y per section 17. Focus and keyboard: every interactive element has visible focus-visible treatment, nodes/edges keyboard-focusable via React Flow a11y settings, focus returns predictably after closing dialogs/menus/inspectors, graph has accessible name 'Workflow canvas'. Screen readers: nodes announce title/category/validation/run state, edges announce source/target/type, inspector tabs announce name/active state, polite live region for major run events ('Run started', 'Node X completed', 'Workflow failed at edge Y', 'Run cancelled'). Contrast: all primary/secondary text WCAG AA, category accents supplemental not sole carriers, selected/running/invalid/disabled distinguishable at limited saturation. Reduced motion: respect prefers-reduced-motion, avoid perpetual motion except active state, replace moving dashes/shimmer with static highlight. Hit targets: resize handles min 10px, icon buttons min 32px, port handles larger invisible hit box. Reference: design system section 17.
design: Files: All component files from tasks 3-8
acceptance_criteria: Focus visible on all interactive elements. Screen reader announces node/edge/run states. Live region for run events. WCAG AA contrast on all text. Hit targets meet minimums (10px resize, 32px buttons). prefers-reduced-motion respected. Graph has aria-label 'Workflow canvas'.

title: Typography, Shape, and Spacing Setup
description: Configure typography, shape hierarchy, and spacing system per section 5. TYPOGRAPHY: Geist Sans + Geist Mono (JetBrains Mono fallback). Set up font-sans and font-mono in Tailwind config. Monospace for IDs, timestamps, JSON, payload types, port labels, and telemetry. Base UI density: text-xs and text-sm. Section labels: uppercase, tracking-wide, text-[10px]. Numeric telemetry: tabular numerals (font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums). SHAPE: Panels: rounded-lg only where panel framing helps. Node cards: rounded-md. Inputs, chips, compact controls: rounded-sm to rounded-md. Borders carry hierarchy; shadows are secondary. Noticeable elevation reserved for dragging, popovers, dialogs only. SPACING: Panel rhythm: 12px/16px. Metadata rows and compact chips: 8px. Node internals: 12px. Toolbar height: 48px. Sticky tab bars and headers preserve clear 1px separation lines. Reference: design system section 5.
acceptance_criteria: Both fonts (sans + mono) load correctly. Monospace renders for code/data elements (IDs, timestamps, JSON, port labels). Sans renders for all UI text. Tabular numerals enabled for telemetry. Base density text-xs/text-sm applied.

title: Node Card Component Redesign
description: Implement the full node card design per section 10. ANATOMY: (1) Header row with subtle category accent (2px header line or 3px inset accent bar), icon tinted softly with category color, title, status dot, quick menu button. (2) Subtitle row: node type, model, or provider in font-mono text-[11px] text-muted-foreground. (3) Optional inline badges: modality, preview, validation, stale state. (4) Left and right port rails. (5) Footer metadata row: duration, resolution, cost, warning summary in font-mono text-[11px]. Card width 280px, rounded-md. Category accents at subdued opacity, never full-card fills. ALL 16 VISUAL STATES with Tailwind rules: (1) Default: bg-card border-border, (2) Hover: stronger border (hover:border-foreground/20) and visible handles, (3) Focus-visible: ring-2 ring-ring, (4) Selected: ring-2 ring-primary border-primary/40, (5) Dragging: elevated z-index, stronger shadow (shadow-lg), slight opacity reduction (opacity-95), (6) Disabled: lower contrast (opacity-55), muted handles, no hover, (7) Readonly, (8) Queued: compact amber badge, (9) Running: thin amber left rule plus animated state dot (1200ms ease-in-out pulse, bg-[color:var(--signal)]), (10) Success: compact semantic badge (no full-card green), (11) Warning: compact amber badge or footer chip, (12) Error: destructive border emphasis and error badge, (13) Stale-data: dashed outline and stale badge, (14) Mock-preview-available: compact chip/thumbnail affordance, (15) Blocked-by-invalid-input, (16) Multi-select summary. LOADING/ASYNC: Skeleton subtitle while schema loads, inline footer spinner on config update, 'No preview yet' for preview-capable nodes without output, config changes mark preview/data stale immediately. TOOLTIP CONTENT: Preview badge='Latest mock output available', Stale badge='Output no longer matches current configuration', Warning badge='Non-blocking issue', Blocked state='Resolve validation issues to run this node'. TEST IDS: data-testid='node-card-{nodeId}', data-testid='node-menu-btn-{nodeId}'. DATA ATTRS: data-selected, data-dragging, data-disabled, data-running. Use transition-[border-color,box-shadow,opacity,transform] duration-150 ease-out. Reference: design system section 10, includes full Tailwind example.
acceptance_criteria: All 15 states render correctly per spec. Category accent colors match spec (slate/blue/violet/teal/amber/gray/emerald). data-testid='node-card-{nodeId}' present. data-selected, data-dragging, data-disabled, data-running attributes present. Port rails functional. Footer metadata displays. Tooltip content matches spec.

title: Typed Edge Component Redesign
description: Implement edge design per section 11. RULES: Neutral edges by default, line style first then color second, permanent edge colors quiet, rely on label pills/hover/execution traces for emphasis, distinguish control flow from data flow even in grayscale. TYPE ENCODING PILLS: VIDEO=amber pill (border-amber-400/40 text-amber-200), AUDIO=teal pill (border-teal-400/40 text-teal-200), IMAGE/FRAME=blue-cyan pill, DATA/JSON=violet pill (border-violet-400/40 text-violet-200), CONTROL=neutral dashed treatment (strokeDasharray '6 4'). Pill styling: rounded-sm border border-border bg-card/95 px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono text-[10px] uppercase tracking-wide text-muted-foreground shadow-sm backdrop-blur. ALL 12 EDGE STATES: (1) Default: 1.5px neutral stroke (stroke-border/80), (2) Hover: brighter edge and label reveal (stroke-foreground/70), (3) Selected: 2px primary stroke (stroke-primary), (4) Valid connection preview: primary/cyan ghost path, (5) Invalid connection preview: destructive dotted path with explanatory tooltip (stroke-destructive), (6) Active execution: animated amber/cyan trace (stroke-[color:var(--signal)], 900ms linear repeating), (7) Success settle: brief semantic flash then return to neutral, (8) Error: destructive marker or midpoint icon, (9) Disabled: low-opacity dash, (10) Blocked: low-opacity dash, (11) Readonly, (12) Edge-selected. Transition: transition-[stroke,stroke-width,opacity] duration-150 ease-out. TOOLTIP CONTENT: Valid target='Compatible: VIDEO -> VIDEO', Invalid target='Incompatible: expected AUDIO', Blocked edge='Upstream node has blocking validation issues', Error marker='Last run failed here'. TEST IDS: data-testid='edge-{edgeId}', data-testid='edge-label-{edgeId}'. DATA ATTRS: data-hovered, data-selected, data-invalid, data-running. Reference: design system section 11, includes BaseEdge and pill Tailwind examples.
acceptance_criteria: All edge states render correctly. Type pills (VIDEO/AUDIO/IMAGE/DATA/CONTROL) display with correct colors. Animation on execution trace (900ms linear). data-testid='edge-{edgeId}' present. data-hovered, data-selected, data-invalid, data-running attributes. Tooltip content matches spec.

title: Inspector Panel Redesign
description: Implement inspector shell per section 12. Fixed-width right panel (400px default, 320px min, 480px max). Sticky header showing selected item title and ID. Sticky tab list with 5 tabs: Config (grouped fields: Inputs/Runtime/Model/Output/Advanced, sticky footer with Apply/Reset/Run Node), Preview (16:9 panel, transport/zoom, stale preview dimmed), Data (Input/Output/Context subviews, monospace JSON, copy/download/filter/collapse), Validation (summary row, group by severity, jump targets), Metadata (two-column key-value, copyable values). acceptance_criteria: All 5 tabs functional per spec. Sticky header shows selection. Panel resize works (320-480px). 8 common states handled. data-testid='inspector' and data-testid='inspector-tab-{tabName}' present. No layout shift on selection change. Dirty state visible in header and footer.

title: Run Toolbar Redesign

THREE-ZONE LAYOUT: Left (workflow name truncated text-sm font-medium, dirty state badge, environment tag), Center (segmented run actions: Run Workflow=primary button bg-primary text-primary-foreground, Run Node=secondary border-input bg-secondary, Run From Here=secondary, Cancel=visible only during active run), Right (status chip in font-mono text-[11px] text-muted-foreground, timer, last-run/active-run ID). ALL 12 STATES: (1) Idle, (2) Ready, (3) Partial-selection, (4) Running-workflow, (5) Running-single-node, (6) Running-from-here, (7) Cancelling, (8) Success, (9) Warning, (10) Failed, (11) Readonly, (12) Dirty-with-unsaved-changes. title: Empty, Loading, and Error States
description: Implement all states per section 14. Empty states: canvas first use (dashed dropzone 'Add first node'), no node selected (compact inspector guidance), no preview yet (16:9 muted frame), no data yet (muted code surface), no validation issues (one-line positive), empty library search (icon + 'Clear filters'), no execution history. Loading states: library item skeletons, inspector form skeletons (sticky header visible), preview skeleton (preserved aspect ratio), data code-block skeleton, validation recompute placeholder, run startup 'Starting...', cancelling 'Stopping...'. Error states: preview error with retry, data load error, validation service error (non-blocking alert), save snapshot error (toast + warning chip), export error (inline destructive alert with retry). Reference: design system section 14.
acceptance_criteria: Every empty state has designed treatment per spec. Loading skeletons preserve layout. Error states show retry affordance. data-testid='canvas-empty-cta' present. Skeleton shimmer 1400ms linear. Interrupted nodes distinct from failed nodes during cancellation.

title: Z-Index and Layering System
description: Implement z-index stacking per section 7. Define CSS custom properties or Tailwind utilities for ALL 11 LAYERS: (1) Canvas background and grid: z-0, (2) Base edges: z-[1], (3) Hovered/selected edges and connection previews: z-[2], (4) Nodes: z-[3], (5) Selected or dragging nodes: z-[4], (6) Edge labels and inline type badges: z-[5], (7) Resize handles and canvas-local overlays: z-10, (8) Dropdowns, context menus, popovers: z-20, (9) Tooltips: z-30, (10) Dialog scrim and modal surfaces: z-40, (11) Toasts: z-50. LAYERING RULES: Tooltips must always sit above menus and canvas overlays. Edge labels should never block port interaction. Drag ghosts appear above nodes and edges, below dialogs. Connection preview lines sit above base edges, below popovers and tooltips. Reference: design system section 7.
acceptance_criteria: All 10 z-index layers defined as CSS custom properties or Tailwind utilities. Tooltips above menus. Drag ghosts below dialogs. Edge labels don't block port interaction. Connection previews between base edges and popovers.

title: Keyboard Shortcuts System
description: Implement all keyboard shortcuts per section 16. THE 12 SHORTCUTS: (1) Cmd/Ctrl+S: Save committed local snapshot — must call preventDefault() so browser does not intercept. (2) Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+E: Export workflow JSON. (3) Cmd/Ctrl+Z: Undo. (4) Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Z: Redo. (5) Backspace/Delete: Delete selection — only graph selection, never text from focused inputs. (6) Space: Pan mode while held — only when canvas owns focus and no text-editing surface is active. (7) A: Quick-add node command menu — not from text inputs, dialog fields, or contenteditable surfaces. (8) Enter: Inspect selected item — only when canvas owns focus and no higher-priority surface is active. (9) R: Run selected node — requires immediate visible feedback in toolbar. (10) Shift+R: Run workflow — requires immediate visible feedback in toolbar. (11) C: Connect from selected node/port via searchable dialog — requires immediate visible feedback in dialog shell. (12) Escape: Clear selection / close menus — closes topmost dismissible UI first, then clears selection if nothing else open. PRECEDENCE RULES (highest to lowest): (1) Open dialogs and modals, (2) Open menus and searchable overlays, (3) Active text inputs/textareas/contenteditable, (4) Inspector body interactions, (5) Library navigation, (6) Canvas-level commands. TEST IDS: data-testid='quick-add-dialog', data-testid='connect-dialog'. Reference: design system section 16.

title: Interaction Patterns Implementation
description: Implement all interaction patterns per section 15. SELECTION MODEL: Click node selects it and updates inspector. Click edge selects edge and may show edge-specific inspector content. Click empty canvas clears selection. Multi-select uses marquee or modifiers, inspector stays anchored to primary item or multi-select summary. Canvas is source of truth for graph selection. DRAG AND DROP: Drag from library into canvas shows placement ghost. Valid drop zones highlight subtly. After drop, node becomes selected and inspector switches to Config tab. Drag ghost preserves icon and node title. PORT HOVER AND CONNECTION PREVIEW: Hovering handle reveals type label and compatibility hint. Dragging from source handle emphasizes compatible targets. Invalid targets dim and show explanatory tooltip on hover. Connection preview line communicates valid vs invalid before release. C opens searchable connect dialog from selected node or port. INSPECTOR SYNCHRONIZATION: Selection changes update inspector immediately without panel jitter. Preview and Data tabs update in place during runs. Changes animate lightly at row/timestamp level, not by replacing entire panels. If selected edge/node disappears, focus recovers cleanly. MOCK EXECUTION PLAYBACK: Traversal animates edge-by-edge and node-by-node in order. Active nodes show running state. Completed nodes settle into success/warning/error badges. Payload updates appear in Data and Preview with timestamps. Cancel marks interrupted nodes differently from failed nodes. Reference: design system section 15.

title: Media Node Preview Pattern
description: Implement the media node preview pattern for nodes that produce visual output. Three preview variants: (1) REFERENCE IMAGE GRID: Grid layout of reference/input images within node card or inspector Preview tab. Thumbnails at consistent aspect ratio, click to expand. Supports 1-N images with overflow indicator. Muted placeholder when no images loaded. (2) IMAGE GENERATION LOADING/COMPLETE: Loading state shows skeleton frame at target aspect ratio with shimmer (1400ms linear). Progress indicator for generation percentage. On complete, thumbnail appears in node card badge area (compact chip). Inspector Preview tab shows full-resolution image in 16:9 panel with metadata (dimensions, model, seed, generation time). Stale preview dimmed with 'Stale preview' chip when config changes. (3) VIDEO COMPOSER WITH 16:9 PREVIEW: 16:9 aspect ratio preview player in inspector Preview tab. Transport controls (play/pause/scrub) and zoom only when preview exists. Timeline scrubber with frame markers. Metadata beneath preview (duration, resolution, frame count, codec). Loading state preserves frame and layout with skeleton. Node card shows compact video duration badge. All preview states follow node card state rules: stale-data shows dashed outline, mock-preview-available shows chip affordance. Use bg-muted for empty preview frames, skeleton shimmer for loading, and subtle border-border frame for content. data-testid patterns: 'node-preview-{nodeId}', 'preview-thumbnail-{nodeId}', 'preview-player-{nodeId}'. Reference: design system sections 10 (Preview available state), 12 (Preview tab).

title: Week 2 Content Strategy & Scripts
description: Continue Linh Vũ's content arc with Week 2 scripts to test different content pillars and validate the production pipeline. Creates 3-5 new video scripts following the HOOK framework with full SAPELTC prompts for image and video generation.

title: Keyboard shortcuts: verify all 12 and add precedence fixes
description:
## File
`src/features/canvas/hooks/use-canvas-shortcuts.ts`

## What Changes
Verify all 12 keyboard shortcuts from design system section 16 are implemented with correct precedence rules. Fix any gaps.

## Shortcut Audit

| Shortcut | Action | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Cmd/Ctrl+S | Save committed local snapshot | ✓ Implemented, calls preventDefault |
| Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+E | Export workflow JSON | ✓ Implemented |
| Cmd/Ctrl+Z | Undo | ✓ Implemented |
| Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Z | Redo | ✓ Implemented |
| Backspace/Delete | Delete selection | ✓ Implemented, only graph selection |
| Space | Pan mode while held | ✓ Implemented with keyup handler |
| A | Quick-add node command menu | ✓ Implemented, blocked in text inputs |
| Enter | Inspect selected item | ✓ Implemented, requires selection |
| R | Run selected node | ✓ Implemented, requires single selection |
| Shift+R | Run workflow | ✓ Implemented |
| C | Connect from selected node | ✓ Implemented, requires single selection |
| Escape | Clear selection / close menus | ✓ Implemented, closes topmost first |

## Precedence Rules Verification
Per design system section 16, precedence order is:
1. Open dialogs and modals → ✓ `isHigherPrioritySurfaceOpen()` checks for `[role="dialog"]`
2. Open menus and searchable overlays → ✓ checks for `[role="menu"]`
3. Active text inputs/textareas/contenteditable → ✓ `isTextInputFocused()` blocks plain keys
4. Inspector body interactions → Need to verify inspector input fields are caught
5. Library navigation → Need to verify library search is caught
6. Canvas-level commands → ✓ fallthrough

## Required Fixes
1. **Inspector body**: Verify that form inputs in `node-config-tab.tsx` are caught by `isTextInputFocused()`. The inspector has `<input>` and `<textarea>` elements that should block plain-key shortcuts. Currently works because `isTextInputFocused` checks `HTMLInputElement` and `HTMLTextAreaElement`. ✓ No fix needed.

2. **Library search**: When the search input in `node-search.tsx` is focused, plain keys (A, R, C) should not fire canvas shortcuts. Currently works because `isTextInputFocused` catches the search input. ✓ No fix needed.

3. **Run feedback**: R and Shift+R "require immediate visible feedback in the toolbar or dialog shell". Currently they dispatch the run which updates the toolbar via store. Verify the toolbar status chip updates within one render cycle. May need to add a brief flash/pulse on the run button when keyboard-triggered.

4. **Space pan mode**: "Only enters pan mode when the canvas owns focus and no text-editing surface is active". Currently checks `isTextInputFocused`. ✓ Correct.

## Acceptance
- All 12 shortcuts work per spec
- Precedence: dialogs > menus > text inputs > inspector > library > canvas
- R and Shift+R show immediate toolbar feedback
- Space only pans when canvas owns focus

title: Accessibility: focus-visible rings, screen reader labels, reduced motion
description:
## Files

## Screen Reader Labels

### Required aria-labels
1. **Canvas**: `aria-label="Workflow canvas"` on ReactFlow wrapper ✓ (canvas-surface.tsx)
2. **Inspector**: `aria-label="Inspector"` on aside element ✓
3. **Node library**: `aria-label="Node templates"` on listbox ✓
4. **Run toolbar**: Need `aria-label="Run toolbar"` on header element — ADD
5. **Node cards**: Complex aria-label with title, disabled state, run status ✓
6. **Tab triggers**: Should announce tab name and active state — shadcn Tabs handles this via role="tab" ✓

### Live Region for Run Events
- Add `aria-live="polite"` region for major run events
- Already implemented in `run-announcer.tsx` ✓ — verify it announces:
  - "Run started"
  - "Node {name} completed"
  - "Workflow failed at edge {id}"
  - "Run cancelled"

## Reduced Motion
Already have in `src/index.css`:
```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  * { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; transition-duration: 80ms !important; }
}
```
✓ This covers the design system requirement.

Additional: verify `animate-execution-trace`, `animate-status-dot`, `animate-shimmer` all respect this.

## WCAG AA Contrast
- Verify `text-muted-foreground` (#8A93A5) on `bg-card` (#11141B) meets 4.5:1 ratio
  - #8A93A5 on #11141B = ~5.2:1 ✓ passes AA

  - Display mode toggle in library: `h-6 w-6` (24px) → change to `h-8 w-8` (32px)

- `--duration-focus: 150ms` → `ease-out`
- `--duration-tab: 160ms` → `ease-out`
- `--duration-pulse: 180ms` → `ease-out`
- `--duration-execution: 900ms` → `linear` repeating
- `--duration-status-dot: 1200ms` → `ease-in-out` repeating
- `--duration-shimmer: 1400ms` → `linear` repeating

### Utility Classes (src/index.css) — Already Defined ✓
- `.transition-hover` → all 120ms ease-out
- `.transition-focus` → all 150ms ease-out
- `.transition-tab` → all 160ms ease-out
- `.transition-pulse` → all 180ms ease-out
- `.animate-execution-trace` → 900ms linear infinite
- `.animate-status-dot` → 1200ms ease-in-out infinite
- `.animate-shimmer` → 1400ms linear infinite

### Component Usage Audit

1. **Node cards** (workflow-node-card.tsx):
   - Currently: `transition-[border-color,box-shadow,opacity,transform] duration-150 ease-out`
   - Should use: `transition-focus` or keep explicit 150ms (matches --duration-focus) ✓

2. **Node library items** (node-library-item.tsx):
   - Currently: `duration-150 ease-out`
   - Should be: `transition-focus` (150ms) ✓

3. **Edge pills** (workflow-edge.tsx):
   - Currently: `duration-150 ease-out`
   - Correct for selection transitions ✓

4. **Tab content** (inspector-panel.tsx):
   - TabsTrigger: `transition-tab` (160ms) ✓

5. **Quick menu button** (workflow-node-card.tsx):
   - Currently: `transition-hover` ✓ (120ms)

6. **Search input** (node-search.tsx):
   - Currently: `transition-hover` ✓

7. **Drag handle** (node-library-item.tsx):
   - Currently: `transition-hover` ✓

### Missing Transition Applications
- **Inspector data refresh**: Should pulse when data updates (180ms ease-out)
  - Add `transition-pulse` to payload viewer when data source changes
  - File: `src/features/data-inspector/components/payload-viewer.tsx`

- **Success settle**: After execution completes, success badge should briefly flash then fade
  - Add a 200ms opacity transition on status badges in node cards

### Execution Edge Animation
- `stroke-dasharray: 12 8` with animated `stroke-dashoffset`
- Currently defined as `animation: execution-trace 900ms linear infinite` ✓
- Verify the `@keyframes execution-trace` offset values (24 → 0) match the dash pattern

## Acceptance
- Hover transitions are exactly 120ms ease-out
- Focus/selection transitions are exactly 150ms ease-out
- Tab content transitions are 160ms ease-out
- Execution trace is 900ms linear repeating
- Status dot pulse is 1200ms ease-in-out repeating
- All components use the utility classes consistently

### "Add first node" Button
- Variant: `outline`
- `gap-2` with Plus icon

### Keyboard Shortcuts Section
- Section label: `text-[10px] uppercase tracking-wide text-muted-foreground font-medium`
- Keys: `font-mono text-[9px] bg-muted rounded px-1` -- already correct

## Acceptance

title: Inspector empty state: no-selection guidance
description:
## File
`src/features/inspector/components/inspector-panel.tsx`

## What Changes
When no node is selected, show a clean empty state matching .pen: "Select a node to inspect" with a pointer/mouse-pointer icon centered in the panel.

## Exact Specs

### No Selection Empty State
Currently when no node is selected, the inspector shows the DataInspectorPanel. Per .pen, it should show a centered guidance message:
- Icon: `MousePointer` from lucide, `h-8 w-8 text-muted-foreground/40` (subtle, not dominant)
- Text: "Select a node to inspect"
- Font: `text-sm text-muted-foreground`
- Layout: `flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-3 h-full`
- Secondary text: "Click a node on the canvas or use keyboard navigation" in `text-xs text-muted-foreground/70`

### Implementation
Replace the current no-selection branch that renders `<DataInspectorPanel />` with the empty state UI. The DataInspectorPanel should only show when a node or edge is selected (which it already checks internally).

## Acceptance
- No selection shows centered pointer icon + guidance text
- Text is #8A93A5 (muted-foreground)
- Icon is subtle at 40% opacity

title: E2E Test Suite
description:
Comprehensive Playwright E2E test suite. 18 tasks across 5 phases: infrastructure (config, page objects, helpers, fixtures), 6 journey tests, 4 scenario tests, keyboard/media/edge-case tests, multi-tab test.

## RULES
1. Read AGENTS.md rules 10-11 about testing before writing any test
2. All tests use data-testid selectors from design system section 18
3. Test files go in src/tests/e2e/specs/
4. Use page objects from src/tests/e2e/pages/ — never raw selectors in specs
5. Run E2E tests with: npx playwright test src/tests/e2e/specs/your-spec.spec.ts
6. NEVER run full suite — always target specific spec file
7. Each test must have @tags in title for filtering
8. Runtime budget: full suite under 2 minutes
9. Viewport: 1440x900 (matches .pen design)

title: Playwright config and project setup
description: Create src/tests/e2e/playwright.config.ts. Config: testDir=./specs, timeout=30000, expect.timeout=5000, fullyParallel=true, retries=CI?2:0, baseURL=http://127.0.0.1:5173, trace=retain-on-failure, screenshot=only-on-failure. webServer: command='npm run dev -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5173', reuseExistingServer=\!CI, timeout=30000. Projects: chromium with Desktop Chrome devices. Add npm run test:e2e script to package.json. Tags: @infrastructure

title: Journey 6: Recovery after refresh
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/journeys/journey-refresh-recovery.spec.ts. Tags: @journey @recovery. Test name: 'Journey 6: recover after refresh @journey @recovery'. Preconditions: Seed dirty draft and interrupted run state via seedRecoveryState(context, dirtyDraftState) using page.addInitScript() to inject localStorage with autosave timestamp, graph state, and interrupted run metadata. Steps: (1) Seed state before navigation: use page.addInitScript() to set localStorage keys for dirty draft + interrupted run. (2) Navigate/reload: page.goto('/') or page.reload(). (3) Assert recovery dialog visible: expect(page.getByTestId('recovery-dialog') or page.getByRole('dialog')).toBeVisible(). (4) Assert dialog content: autosave timestamp shown (expect text matching date/time pattern), interrupted run badge visible, workflow name displayed. (5) Click Restore draft: page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Restore draft' }).click(). (6) Assert workflow restored: nodes from seeded graph state are visible on canvas, edge connections intact, run metadata shows interrupted status (not false success). (7) Assert recovery dialog dismissed: expect(page.getByRole('dialog')).not.toBeVisible(). (8) Cleanup: clear localStorage via page.evaluate(() => localStorage.clear()). Selectors: recovery-dialog (or role=dialog), node-card-{nodeId}, run-status-chip. Playwright APIs: page.addInitScript(), page.reload(), getByRole('button'), toBeVisible(), not.toBeVisible(). Design ref: .pen recovery dialog screens.

title: Scenarios 2-3: Reject incompatible connection and insert adapter
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/scenarios/scenario-reject-incompatible-insert-adapter.spec.ts. Tags: @scenario. Two test cases in one spec file. TEST 1: 'Scenario 2: reject an incompatible connection @scenario'. Steps: (1) seedWorkflow(page, 'emptyWorkflow'). (2) dragNodeToCanvas(page, 'Script Writer', {x:600, y:180}). (3) dragNodeToCanvas(page, 'Video Composer', {x:980, y:180}). (4) connectPorts(page, {sourceNodeId:'script-writer-1', sourcePortKey:'script', targetNodeId:'video-composer-1', targetPortKey:'assets'}). (5) Assert invalid: expect(getByTestId('edge-script-writer-1__video-composer-1')).toHaveAttribute('data-invalid', 'true'). (6) Click edge, open validation tab: getByTestId('inspector-tab-validation').click(). (7) Assert compatibility: expect(getByText('Compatibility result')).toBeVisible(), expect(getByText('invalid')).toBeVisible(). TEST 2: 'Scenario 3: insert adapter node to fix connection @scenario'. Steps: (1) From Scenario 2 state (use beforeEach or sequential within describe). (2) Click quick action to Insert Image Asset Mapper. (3) Assert adapter node appears: getByTestId('node-card-image-asset-mapper-1').toBeVisible(). (4) Assert old invalid edge removed from DOM. (5) Assert two new valid edges exist. (6) Assert no data-invalid='true' edges remain: expect(page.locator('[data-invalid="true"]')).toHaveCount(0). Selectors: edge-{edgeId}, node-card-{nodeId}, inspector-tab-validation, node-port-out-{id}-{key}, node-port-in-{id}-{key}, data-invalid attribute. Design ref: .pen validation and adapter insertion screens.

title: Scenarios 6-7: Fail node then fix and rerun
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/scenarios/scenario-fail-fix-rerun.spec.ts. Tags: @scenario. Two test cases. TEST 1: 'Scenario 6: fail a node due to invalid config @scenario'. Steps: (1) Load workflow with Subtitle Formatter node. (2) Set maxCharsPerLine to 60 via inspector config tab: click getByTestId('node-card-subtitle-formatter-1'), click getByTestId('inspector-tab-config'), page.getByLabel('maxCharsPerLine').fill('60'). (3) Run workflow: click getByTestId('run-btn-workflow'). (4) Assert node error: verifyNodeState(page, 'subtitle-formatter-1', 'invalid'). (5) Assert error toast: expect(getByTestId('toast-run-error')).toBeVisible(). (6) Assert validation item: expect(getByTestId('validation-item-config-max-chars')).toBeVisible(). (7) Assert failure status: expect(getByTestId('run-status-chip')).toContainText('Failed') or similar error state. TEST 2: 'Scenario 7: fix config and run from here @scenario'. Steps: (1) From Scenario 6 state. (2) Fix: click getByTestId('inspector-tab-config'), page.getByLabel('maxCharsPerLine').clear(), fill('32'). (3) Select failed node, click getByTestId('run-btn-from-here'). (4) Assert upstream not rerun: upstream nodes retain success state, never show data-running. (5) Assert downstream rerun: subtitle-formatter-1 transitions to success. (6) Assert final: getByTestId('run-status-chip').toContainText('Success'). Selectors: node-card-{nodeId}, inspector-tab-config, run-btn-workflow, run-btn-from-here, run-status-chip, toast-run-error, validation-item-config-max-chars, data-running, data-stale. Design ref: .pen error state and run toolbar screens.

title: Scenario 8: Export and reimport workflow
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/scenarios/scenario-export-reimport.spec.ts. Tags: @scenario. Test name: 'Scenario 8: export and reimport workflow @scenario'. Steps: (1) Load validShortFormWorkflow via seedWorkflow. (2) Export: click getByTestId('workflow-export-btn') or use keyboard shortcut Cmd+Shift+E via pressShortcut helper. (3) Capture download: const download = await page.waitForEvent('download'); const filePath = await download.path(); const content = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')). (4) Assert export content: verify nodes array length equals 6, edges array length equals 5, workflow name present. (5) Clear canvas or navigate to fresh state. (6) Reimport: trigger the app import flow (file input or drag-drop depending on UI). Use page.setInputFiles() on the file input element if available. (7) Assert round-trip: page.locator('[data-testid^="node-card-"]').count() equals 6, edge count equals 5, verify a representative config field (e.g., sceneCountTarget) survived the round-trip via inspector check. Selectors: workflow-export-btn, node-card-{nodeId}, inspector-tab-config. Playwright APIs: page.waitForEvent('download'), download.path(), page.setInputFiles(). Design ref: .pen toolbar export button.

title: Scenarios 9-10: Recovery — dirty edit and active run
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/scenarios/scenario-recovery-dirty-and-active.spec.ts. Tags: @scenario @recovery. Two test cases. TEST 1: 'Scenario 9: refresh during dirty edit and recover draft @scenario @recovery'. Steps: (1) Load workflow, make a dirty change (add a node or edit config). (2) Assert dirty indicator: expect(getByTestId('workflow-dirty-indicator')).toBeVisible(). (3) Reload: page.reload(). (4) Assert recovery modal: expect(page.getByRole('dialog')).toBeVisible() or getByTestId('recovery-dialog'). (5) Click Restore draft: page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Restore draft' }).click(). (6) Assert unsaved changes returned: the added node or edited config is present on canvas. TEST 2: 'Scenario 10: refresh during active run and recover interrupted state @scenario @recovery'. Steps: (1) Load workflow and start run: click getByTestId('run-btn-workflow'). (2) Wait until at least one node running: await expect(page.locator('[data-running="true"]')).toHaveCount(1, { timeout: 5000 }) or use expect.poll. (3) Reload mid-run: page.reload(). (4) Assert interrupted run badge in recovery modal: recovery dialog shows interrupted run indicator (badge or text). (5) Click Restore draft. (6) Assert restored state shows interruption, not false success: run-status-chip should not say 'Success', nodes should show appropriate stale/interrupted state. Selectors: workflow-dirty-indicator, recovery-dialog, run-btn-workflow, run-status-chip, data-running attribute, node-card-{nodeId}. Playwright APIs: page.reload(), getByRole('dialog'), getByRole('button'), toBeVisible(), toHaveAttribute(). Design ref: .pen recovery dialog and dirty indicator screens.

title: Keyboard shortcuts
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/shortcuts/keyboard-shortcuts.spec.ts. Tags: @shortcut. Covers all 12 shortcuts and precedence rules. Platform detection: const modifier = process.platform === 'darwin' ? 'Meta' : 'Control'. TESTS: (1) 'save and export shortcuts @shortcut' — Cmd+S: press modifier+S, assert getByTestId('workflow-dirty-indicator') clears or save confirmation; Cmd+Shift+E: press modifier+Shift+E, assert page.waitForEvent('download') triggers. (2) 'undo and redo shortcuts @shortcut' — Cmd+Z: make change, press modifier+Z, assert change reverted; Cmd+Shift+Z: press modifier+Shift+Z, assert change reapplied; Delete/Backspace: select node, press Delete, assert node-card removed from DOM. (3) 'quick-add, connect, inspect shortcuts @shortcut' — A: press 'A', assert getByTestId('quick-add-dialog').toBeVisible(); C: press 'C', assert getByTestId('connect-dialog').toBeVisible(); Enter: select node, press Enter, assert getByTestId('inspector').toBeVisible(). (4) 'run shortcuts @shortcut' — R: select node, press 'R', assert run-btn-node triggered / node runs; Shift+R: press Shift+R, assert run-btn-workflow triggered / workflow runs. (5) 'Space for pan mode @shortcut' — press Space, assert canvas enters pan mode (cursor change or pan state). (6) 'Escape precedence @shortcut' — open quick-add dialog (A), press Escape, assert dialog closes first; open connect dialog, press Escape, assert it closes; with node selected, press Escape, assert selection cleared (data-selected becomes false). Topmost UI closes first. (7) 'shortcuts suppressed in text inputs @shortcut' — focus on getByTestId('node-search-input') or getByLabel('sceneCountTarget'), press 'A', assert quick-add-dialog does NOT open; press Delete, assert no node deleted. Selectors: workflow-dirty-indicator, quick-add-dialog, connect-dialog, inspector, node-card-{nodeId}, node-search-input, run-status-chip, data-selected. Playwright APIs: page.keyboard.press(), page.mouse.click() to blur, toBeVisible(), not.toBeVisible(), toHaveAttribute(). Design ref: .pen keyboard shortcuts table (design system section 7).

title: Media previews
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/media/media-preview.spec.ts. Tags: @media. Tests for media preview rendering after mock execution and all empty states. TESTS: (1) 'Image Generator shows 4 thumbnails after mock run @media' — Load validShortFormWorkflow, run workflow, assert getByTestId('node-card-image-generator-1') contains thumbnail grid, assert grid has 4 visible img elements: page.getByTestId('node-card-image-generator-1').locator('img').count() equals 4. Assert overflow badge +N visible when more than 4 images: locator('.overflow-badge') or getByText('+') pattern. (2) 'Video Composer shows poster, play, and timeline @media' — After mock run, assert getByTestId('node-card-video-composer-1') shows: poster frame visible (img or video poster), play button overlay visible, timeline text '0:00 / 0:30' via toContainText(), metadata footer exists. (3) 'Reference Images show imported thumbnails before run @media' — If Reference Images node exists, assert thumbnails display without needing execution. (4) 'Canvas empty state @media' — Fresh app, assert: getByText('Create your first workflow').toBeVisible(), 3 template cards visible, getByText('Add first node') or getByTestId('canvas-empty-cta').toBeVisible(). (5) 'Inspector empty state @media' — No node selected, assert getByTestId('inspector') contains 'Select a node to inspect'. (6) 'Preview empty state @media' — Select node before run, switch to preview tab, assert 'Run this node in mock mode' text. (7) 'Validation empty state @media' — Select valid node, switch to validation tab, assert 'No blocking issues' text. Selectors: node-card-image-generator-1, node-card-video-composer-1, canvas-empty-cta, inspector, inspector-tab-preview, inspector-tab-validation. Playwright APIs: locator('img').count(), toContainText(), toBeVisible(), getByText(). Design ref: .pen media preview and empty state screens (design system sections 5, 9).

title: Edge cases
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/edge-cases/workflow-edge-cases.spec.ts. Tags: @edge-case. TESTS: (1) 'browser back/forward does not corrupt graph @edge-case' — Load workflow, navigate away (page.goto('/other') or history.pushState), go back (page.goBack()), assert node count unchanged, assert selected node state not corrupted, assert no phantom edges. (2) 'rapid run clicks do not trigger duplicate runs @edge-case' — Click getByTestId('run-btn-workflow') 3 times rapidly using Promise.all or sequential clicks within 100ms. Assert only one run executes: run-status-chip shows single Running state, no duplicate toast-run-error or double execution artifacts. (3) 'delete during run is blocked or deferred @edge-case' — Start run, while data-running='true' on a node, select that node, press Delete. Assert either: deletion is blocked (node still visible), or a confirmation dialog appears, or deletion is deferred until run completes. The node must not silently disappear mid-execution. (4) 'undo after edge insertion reverts coherently @edge-case' — Connect two ports via connectPorts(), immediately press Cmd+Z (modifier+Z). Assert edge removed, assert no orphan connections remain, assert workflow-dirty-indicator reflects the undo. (5) 'panel resize does not break controls @edge-case' — If panel resize handles exist (getByTestId('panel-resize-handle')), drag to shrink panels. Assert key controls (run-btn-workflow, inspector tabs, node-search-input) remain visible and clickable. Assert canvas drag/connect still works after resize. (6) 'refresh during open dialog does not restore broken UI @edge-case' — Open quick-add dialog (press A), reload page. Assert on reload: quick-add-dialog is NOT visible (dialogs are ephemeral), canvas loads normally. Selectors: run-btn-workflow, run-status-chip, toast-run-error, node-card-{nodeId}, workflow-dirty-indicator, quick-add-dialog, inspector-tab-{name}, node-search-input, panel-resize-handle, data-running attribute. Playwright APIs: page.goBack(), page.keyboard.press(), toBeVisible(), not.toBeVisible(), toHaveCount(), locator().dragTo(). Design ref: .pen layout and interaction pattern screens.

title: Scenarios 11-12: Multi-tab soft-lock
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/scenarios/scenario-multitab-softlock.spec.ts. Tags: @multitab @slow. Two test cases using Playwright BrowserContext for multi-tab. TEST 1: 'Scenario 11: open same workflow in two tabs shows soft-lock warning @multitab @slow'. Steps: (1) Open first tab with workflow: const page1 = await context.newPage(); await page1.goto('/'); await seedWorkflow(page1, 'validShortFormWorkflow'). (2) Open second tab: const page2 = await context.newPage(); await page2.goto('/'). (3) Assert warning on second tab: await expect(page2.locator('[data-testid="soft-lock-warning"]') or page2.getByText('workflow is open in another tab')).toBeVisible(). (4) Assert warning includes workflow/session context info. TEST 2: 'Scenario 12: close one tab clears warning after heartbeat expiry @multitab @slow'. Steps: (1) From Scenario 11 state. (2) Close first tab: await page1.close(). (3) Wait for heartbeat expiry: use page2.waitForTimeout(heartbeatInterval) or expect.poll with timeout — note: this is one of the few cases where waitForTimeout is acceptable since we are waiting for a real timer. (4) Assert warning clears: await expect(page2.locator('[data-testid="soft-lock-warning"]')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 10000 }). Implementation notes: Use shared BrowserContext (not separate browsers) to share cookies/storage. Mark test as @slow since heartbeat expiry may take several seconds. Consider using page.evaluate to accelerate timer if app exposes a test hook. Selectors: soft-lock-warning or similar warning testid, node-card-{nodeId}. Playwright APIs: context.newPage(), page.close(), expect().not.toBeVisible(), waitForTimeout(). Design ref: .pen multi-tab warning screens.

title: Page objects for UI surfaces
description: Create 5 page objects in src/tests/e2e/pages/. Files: (1) app-shell.page.ts — AppShellPage: waitUntilReady() waits for app ready state via page.waitForSelector('[data-testid="canvas-empty-cta"]') or loaded indicator; detectDialogs() checks for recovery-dialog, quick-add-dialog, connect-dialog; openTemplate(name) clicks template card. (2) canvas.page.ts — CanvasPage: dragNode(label, point) uses page.getByTestId('node-search-input') and dragTo; selectNode(nodeId) clicks getByTestId('node-card-{nodeId}'); connectPorts({sourceNodeId, sourcePortKey, targetNodeId, targetPortKey}) uses getByTestId('node-port-out-{sourceNodeId}-{sourcePortKey}').dragTo(getByTestId('node-port-in-...')); deleteSelection() presses Delete/Backspace; selectEdge(edgeId) clicks getByTestId('edge-{edgeId}'). (3) inspector.page.ts — InspectorPage: switchTab(tabName) clicks getByTestId('inspector-tab-{tabName}') for config/preview/data/validation/meta; editConfig(label, value) uses page.getByLabel(label).fill(value); assertPayload(expected) checks inspector data tab content; assertEmpty() checks 'Select a node to inspect'. (4) toolbar.page.ts — ToolbarPage: runWorkflow() clicks getByTestId('run-btn-workflow'); runSelectedNode() clicks getByTestId('run-btn-node'); runFromHere() clicks getByTestId('run-btn-from-here'); cancel() clicks getByTestId('run-btn-cancel'); save() clicks getByTestId('workflow-save-btn'); export() clicks getByTestId('workflow-export-btn'); readStatusChip() returns text from getByTestId('run-status-chip'). (5) recovery-dialog.page.ts — RecoveryDialogPage: assertTimestamp() checks autosave time; assertRunBadge() checks interrupted run badge; assertWorkflowName(name); clickRestore() clicks 'Restore draft' via getByRole('button'); clickDiscard(); clickStartFresh(). Acceptance: All 5 page objects created, each exports a class, uses data-testid selectors from design system section 18. Tags: @infrastructure

title: Helper functions for test actions
description: Create helper modules in src/tests/e2e/helpers/. Files: (1) canvas.ts — dragNodeToCanvas(page, nodeLabel, point:{x,y}): searches node library via getByTestId('node-search-input'), finds library item, uses dragTo() to canvas coordinates; connectPorts(page, {sourceNodeId, sourcePortKey, targetNodeId, targetPortKey}): locates getByTestId('node-port-out-{src}-{key}').dragTo(getByTestId('node-port-in-{tgt}-{key}')). (2) workflow.ts — runWorkflow(page): clicks getByTestId('run-btn-workflow'), waits for run-status-chip to show 'Running'; runSelectedNode(page): clicks getByTestId('run-btn-node'); runFromHere(page): clicks getByTestId('run-btn-from-here'); seedWorkflow(page, fixtureName): injects workflow via page.addInitScript() or page.route() with deterministic JSON; seedRecoveryState(context, state): seeds localStorage with dirty draft or running workflow state. (3) inspector.ts — selectInspectorTab(page, tabName): clicks getByTestId('inspector-tab-{tabName}'); verifyPayload(page, expected): switches to data tab, asserts JSON content matches expected shape; inspectEdge(page, edgeId): clicks getByTestId('edge-{edgeId}'), opens data tab. (4) keyboard.ts — pressShortcut(page, shortcut): platform-aware keyboard helper, uses process.platform==='darwin'?'Meta':'Control' for modifier keys; handles Cmd+S, Cmd+Z, Cmd+Shift+E, Shift+R, etc. (5) assertions.ts — verifyNodeState(page, nodeId, state): expect(getByTestId('node-card-{nodeId}')).toHaveAttribute('data-{state}','true') for selected/running/invalid/stale; verifyMediaPreview(page, nodeId, kind): checks thumbnail grid for images, poster+play for video; verifyEdgeState(page, edgeId, state): checks data-invalid, data-selected on edge elements. Acceptance: All 5 helper modules created, exported functions typed with Page parameter, uses Playwright API (getByTestId, dragTo, toHaveAttribute, toBeVisible). Tags: @infrastructure

title: Fixtures and test data
description: Create fixture and test data files in src/tests/e2e/fixtures/. Files: (1) test.ts — Custom Playwright fixture extending base test with AppShellPage: test = base.extend<{app: AppShellPage}>({ app: async ({page}, use) => { const app = new AppShellPage(page); await page.goto('/'); await app.waitUntilReady(); await use(app); }}). (2) workflows.ts — Workflow fixture data: emptyWorkflow (fresh empty canvas state), validShortFormWorkflow (6 nodes: User Prompt, Script Writer, Scene Splitter, Image Generator, Video Composer, Final Export with 5 edges fully connected), savedBrokenConnectionWorkflow (workflow with script-writer output incorrectly connected to video-composer input, data-invalid edge), failedSubtitleWorkflow (workflow where Subtitle Formatter has maxCharsPerLine=60 causing validation failure), templateNarratedProductTeaser (9 pre-connected nodes matching Narrated Product Teaser template). Each fixture is a JSON-serializable graph with nodes[], edges[], and config. (3) payloads.ts — Expected payload shapes per edge: scriptWriterToSceneSplitterPayload (script text + metadata), sceneSplitterToImageGeneratorPayload (scenes array with prompts), imageGeneratorToVideoComposerPayload (image URLs + dimensions), videoComposerToFinalExportPayload (video URL + duration + format). Each payload has a schema field and data field for assertion matching. (4) recovery.ts — Recovery state fixtures: dirtyDraftState (localStorage with unsaved graph changes + workflow-dirty-indicator=true + autosave timestamp), runningWorkflowState (localStorage with active run metadata + node states mid-execution + interrupted run badge data). Seeded via page.addInitScript() to inject into localStorage before page load. Acceptance: All 4 fixture files created, workflows importable by seedWorkflow helper, payloads match shapes from GPT plan section 3, recovery states seedable via addInitScript. Tags: @infrastructure

title: Journey 1: Create workflow from scratch
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/journeys/journey-create-workflow.spec.ts. Tags: @journey @smoke. Test name: 'Journey 1: create a short-form workflow from scratch @journey @smoke'. Preconditions: App opens on fresh empty canvas, mock execution enabled. Steps: (1) Navigate to '/', assert getByTestId('canvas-empty-cta').toBeVisible() and getByTestId('node-search-input').toBeVisible(). (2) Drag 6 nodes to canvas using dragNodeToCanvas(): User Prompt (420,140), Script Writer (680,140), Scene Splitter (940,140), Image Generator (1200,140), Video Composer (1460,140), Final Export (1720,140). (3) Connect 5 edges using connectPorts(): user-prompt-1/prompt -> script-writer-1/prompt, script-writer-1/script -> scene-splitter-1/script, scene-splitter-1/scenes -> image-generator-1/scenes, image-generator-1/images -> video-composer-1/assets, video-composer-1/video -> final-export-1/video. (4) Configure: click getByTestId('node-card-scene-splitter-1'), assert getByTestId('inspector').toBeVisible(), click getByTestId('inspector-tab-config'), fill getByLabel('sceneCountTarget') with '6', switch to preview tab, assert getByText('6 scenes').toBeVisible(). (5) Run: click getByTestId('run-btn-workflow'), assert getByTestId('run-status-chip').toContainText('Running'), verifyNodeState(page, 'script-writer-1', 'running'), wait for getByTestId('run-status-chip').toContainText('Success'). (6) Inspect edge: click getByTestId('edge-script-writer-1__scene-splitter-1'), click getByTestId('inspector-tab-data'), assert getByText('payload').toBeVisible(), assert getByText('schema').toBeVisible(). Assertions: canvas-empty-cta disappears after first node, inspector opens for selected node, preview reflects sceneCountTarget=6, run transitions idle->running->success, edge data inspectable after run. Selectors used: canvas-empty-cta, node-search-input, node-card-{nodeId}, node-port-out-{id}-{key}, node-port-in-{id}-{key}, inspector, inspector-tab-config, inspector-tab-preview, inspector-tab-data, run-btn-workflow, run-status-chip, edge-{edgeId}. Design ref: .pen canvas and inspector screens.

title: Journey 2: Diagnose broken connection
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/journeys/journey-diagnose-broken-connection.spec.ts. Tags: @journey. Test name: 'Journey 2: diagnose a broken connection @journey'. Preconditions: Load savedBrokenConnectionWorkflow via seedWorkflow(page, 'savedBrokenConnectionWorkflow'). Steps: (1) Select the invalid edge: click getByTestId('edge-script-writer-1__video-composer-1'). (2) Assert invalid state: expect(getByTestId('edge-script-writer-1__video-composer-1')).toHaveAttribute('data-invalid', 'true'). (3) Open validation tab: click getByTestId('inspector-tab-validation'). (4) Assert inspector shows: source schema, target schema, compatibility result with 'invalid' text, explanation text, suggested fixes list. Use expect(getByText('Compatibility result')).toBeVisible() and expect(getByText('invalid')).toBeVisible(). (5) Quick fix: click suggested fix action 'Insert Image Asset Mapper'. (6) Assert adapter insertion: new node 'image-asset-mapper-1' appears via getByTestId('node-card-image-asset-mapper-1').toBeVisible(). (7) Assert old invalid edge removed: getByTestId('edge-script-writer-1__video-composer-1') no longer in DOM. (8) Assert two new valid edges exist without data-invalid='true'. (9) Verify no remaining invalid edges: page.locator('[data-invalid="true"]').count() === 0. Selectors: edge-{edgeId}, inspector-tab-validation, node-card-{nodeId}, validation-item-{issueCode}, data-invalid attribute. Design ref: .pen validation and edge inspector screens.

title: Journey 3: Rerun from failed step
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/journeys/journey-rerun-from-failed-step.spec.ts. Tags: @journey. Test name: 'Journey 3: rerun from a failed step @journey'. Preconditions: Load failedSubtitleWorkflow via seedWorkflow(page, 'failedSubtitleWorkflow'). Steps: (1) Run workflow: click getByTestId('run-btn-workflow'), wait for run to complete. (2) Assert failure: verifyNodeState(page, 'subtitle-formatter-1', 'invalid'), expect toast-run-error to be visible via getByTestId('toast-run-error').toBeVisible(). (3) Assert validation: click getByTestId('node-card-subtitle-formatter-1'), click getByTestId('inspector-tab-validation'), expect(getByTestId('validation-item-config-max-chars')).toBeVisible(). (4) Fix config: click getByTestId('inspector-tab-config'), page.getByLabel('maxCharsPerLine').clear(), page.getByLabel('maxCharsPerLine').fill('32'). (5) Run from here: click getByTestId('run-btn-from-here'). (6) Assert upstream not rerun: upstream nodes (script-writer-1, scene-splitter-1) should NOT show data-running='true' at any point — use expect.poll or verify they retain success state throughout. (7) Assert downstream rerun: subtitle-formatter-1 and downstream nodes transition running->success. (8) Assert final success: getByTestId('run-status-chip').toContainText('Success'). Selectors: run-btn-workflow, run-btn-from-here, run-status-chip, toast-run-error, node-card-{nodeId}, inspector-tab-validation, inspector-tab-config, validation-item-config-max-chars, data-running, data-stale attributes. Design ref: .pen error state and toolbar screens.

title: Journey 4: Inspect edge data
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/journeys/journey-inspect-edge-data.spec.ts. Tags: @journey. Test name: 'Journey 4: inspect data on an edge @journey'. Preconditions: Load validShortFormWorkflow and run it successfully via seedWorkflow + runWorkflow so edge payloads are populated. Use expect(getByTestId('inspector')).toContainText('payload'), expect(getByText('schema')).toBeVisible(). (5) Verify edge label: expect(getByTestId('edge-label-script-writer-1__scene-splitter-1')).toBeVisible(). (6) Optional — test copy action: click copy button, verify clipboard contains expected payload via page.evaluate(() => navigator.clipboard.readText()). (7) Verify target-schema comparison UI is visible. Selectors: edge-{edgeId}, edge-label-{edgeId}, inspector, inspector-tab-data, data-selected attribute. Design ref: .pen data inspector and edge mode screens.

title: Journey 5: Template fork and export
description: Create src/tests/e2e/specs/journeys/journey-template-fork-export.spec.ts. Tags: @journey @smoke. Test name: 'Journey 5: start from template and fork @journey @smoke'. Preconditions: App starts on empty canvas with template cards visible. Steps: (1) Assert empty state: expect(getByTestId('canvas-empty-cta')).toBeVisible(), verify 3 template cards visible.
