Design ref: .pen template cards and empty state screens.

title: [Phase2] Epic: Backend Skeleton & Docker Setup
description: Scaffold the entire Laravel backend infrastructure: Laravel app, Docker Compose environment (4 services), health endpoint, and verify everything works end-to-end. This is the foundation all other steps depend on.

title: [P2-S1] Scaffold Laravel app in backend/
description: Create a new Laravel 11 application in the backend/ directory using composer create-project. Configure composer.json, artisan, .env.example. Set up directory structure with namespaces: app/Domain/, app/Models/, app/Jobs/, app/Events/, app/Http/Controllers/, app/Services/. Configure config/database.php for PostgreSQL (pgsql driver, env-based host/db/user/pass). Configure config/queue.php for Redis. Configure config/broadcasting.php for Redis. WHY: Foundation that all other beads depend on — the Laravel skeleton must exist before any domain code. Tests: php artisan --version succeeds, composer validate passes, directory structure exists.

title: [P2-S1] Docker Compose with 4 services (app, worker, postgres, redis)
description: Create backend/docker-compose.yml with 4 services: app (PHP 8.3-FPM + Nginx on port 8000, volume mounts for source + artifacts), worker (same image, command: php artisan queue:work redis --queue=workflow-runs --timeout=900 --tries=1 --sleep=3), postgres (16-alpine, DB=aimodel, user=aimodel, pass=secret, pgdata volume, port 5432), redis (7-alpine, port 6379). Create backend/docker/Dockerfile with PHP extensions: pdo_pgsql, redis, pcntl. Create backend/docker/nginx.conf for PHP-FPM proxy. Volumes: pgdata, artifact-storage. WHY: Containerized environment ensures consistent dev setup and matches production. Tests: docker compose config validates, docker compose build succeeds.

title: [Phase2] Epic: Domain Enums & Value Objects
description: Implement all domain primitives: 6 PHP enums, 3 value objects (PortDefinition, PortPayload, PortSchema), NodeExecutionContext, and TypeCompatibility checker. These form the type system shared across all node templates and the execution engine.

title: [P2-S1] Health endpoint GET /api/health
description: Add GET /api/health endpoint in app/Http/Controllers/HealthController.php that returns JSON: {status: 'ok', timestamp: ISO8601, services: {database: 'ok'|'error', redis: 'ok'|'error', queue: 'ok'|'error'}}. Register in routes/api.php. Acceptance criteria: docker compose up -d succeeds, curl localhost:8000/api/health returns 200 with all services healthy, worker container is running. Tests: feature test hitting /api/health verifying JSON structure and 200 status.

title: [P2-S1] Verify docker compose up + curl health check
description: Integration verification: run docker compose up -d, wait for services, curl localhost:8000/api/health, verify JSON response shows all services healthy. Document the startup procedure.

title: [P2-S2] Create all 6 domain enums (DataType, NodeCategory, RunStatus, NodeRunStatus, RunTrigger, Capability)
description: Create 6 PHP 8.1+ backed enums in backend/app/Domain/: DataType.php (18 cases: text, textList, prompt, promptList, script, scene, sceneList, imageFrame, imageFrameList, imageAsset, imageAssetList, audioPlan, audioAsset, subtitleAsset, videoAsset, reviewDecision, json), NodeCategory.php (7: input, script, visuals, audio, video, utility, output), RunStatus.php (7: pending, running, awaitingReview, success, error, cancelled, interrupted), NodeRunStatus.php (7: pending, running, awaitingReview, success, error, skipped, cancelled), RunTrigger.php (4: runWorkflow, runNode, runFromHere, runUpToHere), Capability.php (5: text_generation, text_to_image, text_to_speech, structured_transform, media_composition). All use string backing. WHY: Shared type vocabulary used by every layer — templates, execution engine, API responses. Tests: unit test that each enum can be constructed from its string value, DataType::from('text') === DataType::Text, invalid string throws ValueError.

title: [P2-S2] Create PortDefinition, PortPayload, PortSchema value objects
description: Create 3 readonly value objects in backend/app/Domain/: PortDefinition.php (readonly class with: string key, string label, string direction ('input'|'output'), DataType dataType, bool required, bool multiple, ?string description), PortPayload.php (readonly class with: mixed value, string status, DataType schemaType, ?string producedAt, ?string sourceNodeId, ?string sourcePortKey, ?string previewText, ?string previewUrl, ?int sizeBytesEstimate, ?string errorMessage; static factories: success(), error(), idle(), fromArray()), PortSchema.php (readonly class with: array inputs, array outputs — both PortDefinition[]). WHY: These define the port contract shared by all node templates and the execution engine. Tests: unit test PortPayload::success() sets status='success' and producedAt, PortPayload::error() sets errorMessage, PortPayload::fromArray() round-trips correctly, PortSchema holds input/output arrays.

title: [P2-S2] Create NodeExecutionContext
description: Create backend/app/Domain/Nodes/NodeExecutionContext.php — readonly context object passed to every node's execute() method. Constructor: string nodeId, array config, array<string,PortPayload> inputs, string runId, ProviderRouter providerRouter, ArtifactStoreContract artifactStore. Helper methods: input(string key) → ?PortPayload, inputValue(string key) → mixed (shortcut for ->value), provider(Capability) → ProviderContract (delegates to providerRouter->resolve(), passes config for per-node API keys), storeArtifact(string name, string contents, string mimeType) → Artifact (delegates to artifactStore). WHY: Single entry point for node templates to access inputs, providers, and artifact storage. Tests: unit test that input() returns correct PortPayload by key, inputValue() returns the value property, provider() delegates to ProviderRouter with config, storeArtifact() delegates to ArtifactStoreContract.

title: [P2-S2] Implement TypeCompatibility with tests
description: Implement app/Domain/Execution/TypeCompatibility with CompatibilityResult. Rules: exact DataType match = compatible, scalar-to-list wrapping (text→textList, prompt→promptList, scene→sceneList, imageFrame→imageFrameList, imageAsset→imageAssetList) = compatible with coercion warning, list-to-scalar = incompatible error, everything else = incompatible error. Include comprehensive unit tests for all type pairs.

title: [Phase2] Epic: Node Templates & Registry
description: Build the complete node template system: abstract base class, all 11 concrete templates (6 live + 5 stubs), StubAdapter for testing, NodeTemplateRegistry with Laravel service provider auto-registration, and config validation rules with tests.

title: [P2-S3] Create abstract NodeTemplate base class
description: Create backend/app/Domain/Nodes/NodeTemplate.php abstract class. Abstract properties (PHP 8.4 property hooks): string type, string version, string title, NodeCategory category, string description. Abstract methods: ports() → PortSchema, configRules() → array (returns Laravel validation rules array), defaultConfig() → array, execute(NodeExecutionContext) → array (returns PortPayload[] keyed by output port key). Virtual method with default implementation: activePorts(array config) → PortSchema (returns ports() by default; override for config-dependent nodes like ImageGenerator). Single class — no interface hierarchy, no Executable/NonExecutable discrimination. WHY: One unified base class for all 11 node templates. Tests: unit test with a concrete test subclass verifying abstract contract — ports() returns PortSchema, execute() returns keyed output array, activePorts() defaults to ports().

title: [P2-S3] Implement Milestone 1 templates (UserPrompt, ScriptWriter, SceneSplitter, PromptRefiner, ImageGenerator, ReviewCheckpoint)
description: Implement 6 Milestone 1 node templates in backend/app/Domain/Nodes/Templates/: UserPromptTemplate.php (type=userPrompt, category=input, no capability — returns config values as Prompt output, no provider call), ScriptWriterTemplate.php (type=scriptWriter, category=script, TextGeneration — prompt→script with structured JSON response), SceneSplitterTemplate.php (type=sceneSplitter, category=script, TextGeneration — script→sceneList), PromptRefinerTemplate.php (type=promptRefiner, category=script, TextGeneration — sceneList→promptList), ImageGeneratorTemplate.php (type=imageGenerator, category=visuals, TextToImage — config-dependent activePorts with inputMode/outputMode, iterates prompts/scenes and generates images), ReviewCheckpointTemplate.php (type=reviewCheckpoint, category=utility — throws ReviewPendingException to pause execution). Each template must implement ports(), configRules(), defaultConfig(), execute(). Also create backend/app/Domain/Nodes/Exceptions/ReviewPendingException.php. Register all 6 templates in NodeTemplateServiceProvider. WHY: These are the vertical slice — the minimum templates needed for prompt→script→scenes→images pipeline. Tests: unit test each template's execute() with StubAdapter returns correct output structure, test configRules() rejects invalid config, test ImageGenerator activePorts() varies by config.

title: [Phase2] Epic: Database & Models
description: Build the persistence layer: 5 database migrations with indexes, Eloquent models with JSONB casts, WorkflowController with full CRUD, API Resources, and feature tests.

title: [P2-S3] Create NodeTemplateRegistry with service provider registration
description: Create app/Domain/Nodes/NodeTemplateRegistry class: register(NodeTemplate), get(type) → NodeTemplate|null, all() → array, metadata() → array of {type, version, title, category, description, inputs, outputs}. Create NodeTemplateServiceProvider that registers it as a singleton in the container. The service provider starts empty — each template bead (aimodel-557, aimodel-593) registers their own templates in the provider. Tests: unit test for register/get/all/metadata methods with a mock template, test that get() returns null for unknown type.

title: [P2-S4] Create 5 migrations (workflows, execution_runs, node_run_records, run_cache_entries, artifacts)
description: Create 5 PostgreSQL migrations in backend/database/migrations/: 1) create_workflows_table: UUID PK, name varchar(255), description text, schema_version int default 1, tags JSONB default '[]', document JSONB NOT NULL, timestamps; indexes on name, updated_at, GIN on tags. 2) create_execution_runs_table: UUID PK, workflow_id FK CASCADE, mode varchar(20) default 'live', trigger varchar(20), target_node_id varchar(255) nullable, planned_node_ids JSONB default '[]', status varchar(20) default 'pending', document_snapshot JSONB, document_hash varchar(64), node_config_hashes JSONB default '{}', started_at timestamp, completed_at timestamp nullable, termination_reason varchar(30) nullable; indexes on workflow_id, status, started_at. 3) create_node_run_records_table: UUID PK, run_id FK CASCADE, node_id varchar(255), status varchar(20) default 'pending', skip_reason varchar(30) nullable, blocked_by_node_ids JSONB nullable, input_payloads JSONB default '{}', output_payloads JSONB default '{}', error_message text nullable, used_cache bool default false, duration_ms int nullable, started_at timestamp nullable, completed_at timestamp nullable; unique index on run_id+node_id. 4) create_run_cache_entries_table: UUID PK, cache_key varchar(255) UNIQUE, node_type varchar(50), template_version varchar(20), output_payloads JSONB, created_at timestamp, last_accessed_at timestamp; indexes on node_type, last_accessed_at. 5) create_artifacts_table: UUID PK, run_id FK CASCADE, node_id varchar(255), name varchar(255), mime_type varchar(100), size_bytes bigint, disk varchar(20) default 'local', path varchar(500), created_at timestamp; index on run_id. WHY: Persistence layer for workflow execution state. Tests: run php artisan migrate:fresh inside Docker, verify all 5 tables exist with correct columns via pg_catalog query.

title: [P2-S3] Config validation rules per template + tests
description: Test config validation for all 11 templates via the registry. Create a shared validation helper: given a node type and config array, look up the template via NodeTemplateRegistry, get configRules(), run Laravel Validator. Tests: for each template, test that defaultConfig() passes configRules(), test that missing required fields fail validation, test that out-of-range values fail (e.g., ScriptWriter targetDurationSeconds=0 or 9999), test invalid enum values fail (e.g., structure='invalid'). Also test the validation helper returns structured error messages. WHY: Config validation prevents invalid node configs from reaching execute() at runtime.

title: [P2-S4] Create Eloquent models with JSONB casts
description: Create 5 Eloquent models in backend/app/Models/: Workflow.php (UUID PK via HasUuids trait, casts: tags→array, document→array; fillable: name, description, schema_version, tags, document), ExecutionRun.php (UUID PK, casts: planned_node_ids→array, document_snapshot→array, node_config_hashes→array; relationships: belongsTo workflow, hasMany nodeRunRecords; fillable: workflow_id, mode, trigger, target_node_id, planned_node_ids, status, document_snapshot, document_hash, node_config_hashes, started_at, completed_at, termination_reason), NodeRunRecord.php (UUID PK, casts: blocked_by_node_ids→array, input_payloads→array, output_payloads→array; relationship: belongsTo run; fillable: run_id, node_id, status, skip_reason, blocked_by_node_ids, input_payloads, output_payloads, error_message, used_cache, duration_ms, started_at, completed_at), RunCacheEntry.php (UUID PK, casts: output_payloads→array), Artifact.php (UUID PK, relationship: belongsTo run). WHY: ORM layer used by RunExecutor, RunCache, and API controllers. Tests: unit test that JSONB casts round-trip arrays correctly, test relationship loading (ExecutionRun→nodeRunRecords).

title: [P2-S4] Implement WorkflowController CRUD endpoints
description: Create backend/app/Http/Controllers/WorkflowController.php with 5 endpoints registered in routes/api.php: GET /api/workflows (index — paginated via simplePaginate, ?search= name LIKE filter, ?tags= JSONB contains filter), POST /api/workflows (store — validate name required string max:255, document required array, create Workflow, return 201 + WorkflowResource), GET /api/workflows/{id} (show — return WorkflowResource including document), PUT /api/workflows/{id} (update — validate and update document + name, return WorkflowResource), DELETE /api/workflows/{id} (destroy — cascades via FK to runs + artifacts, return 204). All responses use WorkflowResource for consistent format. WHY: CRUD API for frontend to persist and retrieve workflow documents.

title: [P2-S4] Create API Resources for consistent response format
description: Create Laravel API Resources in backend/app/Http/Resources/: WorkflowResource.php (id, name, description, schemaVersion, tags, document, createdAt, updatedAt), ExecutionRunResource.php (id, workflowId, mode, trigger, targetNodeId, plannedNodeIds, status, startedAt, completedAt, terminationReason; conditionally include nodeRunRecords when loaded), NodeRunRecordResource.php (id, runId, nodeId, status, skipReason, inputPayloads, outputPayloads, errorMessage, usedCache, durationMs), ArtifactResource.php (id, runId, nodeId, name, mimeType, sizeBytes, url, createdAt). Use camelCase keys in JSON output. WHY: Consistent JSON envelope across all API responses.

title: [P2-S4] Feature tests for Workflow CRUD
description: Feature tests for WorkflowController CRUD: test create workflow with valid document, test create rejects invalid document, test list with pagination, test list with search filter, test list with tags filter, test show returns full document, test update modifies document, test delete cascades to runs and artifacts, test 404 for missing workflow.

title: [Phase2] Epic: Execution Engine
description: Build the complete execution engine: planner with 4 trigger types, input resolver with 3-priority fallback, payload hasher + run cache, the main RunExecutor loop, queue job, API trigger endpoint, workflow validator, and feature tests for full runs.

title: [P2-S5] Implement ExecutionPlanner with all 4 trigger types + tests
description: Implement app/Domain/Execution/ExecutionPlanner. Method: plan(document, RunTrigger, ?targetNodeId) → ExecutionPlan(orderedNodeIds, skippedNodeIds, trigger, targetNodeId). Logic: 1) compute candidate scope per trigger (RunWorkflow=all, RunNode=target+upstream, RunFromHere=target+downstream, RunUpToHere=target+upstream), 2) prune disabled nodes to skippedIds, 3) topological sort remaining via Kahn's algorithm. Unit tests for all 4 triggers, disabled pruning, cycle detection failure.

title: [P2-S5] Implement InputResolver with priority 1/2/3 resolution + tests
description: Implement app/Domain/Execution/InputResolver. Method: resolve(node, template, document, nodeRunRecords, cache) → [ok, inputs] or [ok=false, reason, blockedBy]. For each active input port: find connected edge, then try 3 priorities: 1) successful upstream output from current run's nodeRunRecords, 2) cache hit via RunCache, 3) preview from non-executable upstream node's document data. Required ports with no resolution → failure. Unit tests for each priority path and missing-input scenarios.

title: [P2-S5] Implement PayloadHasher + RunCache + tests
description: Implement PayloadHasher (app/Domain/Execution/PayloadHasher): hashConfig(array) with recursive key sorting for determinism, hashInputs(array) stripping volatile fields (producedAt, sourceNodeId, sourcePortKey) keeping only value+schemaType+status. Implement RunCache (app/Domain/Execution/RunCache): buildKey(node, template, schemaVersion, inputs) → SHA256 of type:version:schemaVersion:configHash:inputsHash, get(key) queries run_cache_entries, put(key, outputs) upserts. Tests: key determinism, different configs → different keys, cache hit/miss.

title: [P2-S5] Create RunWorkflowJob (queue job)
description: Create backend/app/Jobs/RunWorkflowJob.php implementing ShouldQueue. Use traits: Dispatchable, InteractsWithQueue, Queueable, SerializesModels. Properties: int timeout = 900 (15 minutes), string queue = 'workflow-runs'. Constructor: string runId. handle(RunExecutor executor): load ExecutionRun::findOrFail(runId), call executor->execute(run). failed(Throwable e): find run, update status to RunStatus::Interrupted, set termination_reason='jobFailed', set completed_at, broadcast RunCompleted event. WHY: Decouples HTTP request from execution — runs execute asynchronously via Redis queue. Tests: test that dispatching the job calls RunExecutor::execute(), test failed() sets correct terminal state.

title: [P2-S5] Implement RunController trigger endpoint
description: Create backend/app/Http/Controllers/RunController.php. POST /api/workflows/{id}/runs: validate {trigger: RunTrigger string, targetNodeId?: string}, load workflow, create ExecutionRun record (snapshot document into document_snapshot, compute document_hash via SHA256, compute node_config_hashes per node, set status=pending), dispatch RunWorkflowJob, return 202 with ExecutionRunResource. GET /api/runs/{id}: return ExecutionRun with nodeRunRecords loaded via ExecutionRunResource. Register routes in routes/api.php. WHY: API entry point for triggering and monitoring workflow runs. Tests: feature test POST returns 202 with valid run ID, test GET returns run with nested node records, test invalid trigger value returns 422.

title: [P2-S5] Feature tests for full run with stubs
description: Feature tests for full execution flow using StubAdapter: trigger a RunWorkflow on a multi-node workflow, verify all NodeRunRecords created with correct statuses, verify terminal run status is 'success'. Test RunNode trigger executes only target+upstream. Test error in one node doesn't crash the run. Test cache hit skips re-execution. Test disabled node is skipped.

title: [Phase2] Epic: Streaming, Review & Cancellation
description: Add real-time capabilities: Laravel broadcasting events via Redis, SSE endpoint for live progress streaming, review checkpoint with polling/timeout, and cooperative cancellation. Feature tests for all flows.

title: [P2-S6] Implement broadcasting events (NodeStatusChanged, RunStarted, RunCompleted)
description: Create 3 Laravel broadcast events in backend/app/Events/: RunStarted.php (implements ShouldBroadcast; properties: string runId, string status, array plannedNodeIds; broadcastOn → Channel('run.{runId}'), broadcastAs → 'run.started'), NodeStatusChanged.php (implements ShouldBroadcast; properties: string runId, string nodeId, string status, ?array outputPayloads, ?int durationMs, ?string errorMessage, ?string skipReason, bool usedCache; broadcastOn → Channel('run.{runId}'), broadcastAs → 'node.status'), RunCompleted.php (implements ShouldBroadcast; properties: string runId, string status, ?string terminationReason, ?string completedAt; broadcastOn → Channel('run.{runId}'), broadcastAs → 'run.completed'). Wire BROADCAST_CONNECTION=redis in config/broadcasting.php. WHY: Enables real-time progress updates to the frontend via SSE. Tests: unit test each event serializes to correct JSON shape, test broadcastOn returns correct channel name.

title: [P2-S6] Implement SSE endpoint GET /api/runs/{id}/stream
description: Create backend/app/Http/Controllers/RunStreamController.php with GET /api/runs/{id}/stream endpoint. Returns StreamedResponse with SSE headers (Content-Type: text/event-stream, Cache-Control: no-cache, Connection: keep-alive, X-Accel-Buffering: no). On connect: 1) send run.catchup event with current ExecutionRun + all NodeRunRecords (so late-joining clients sync up). 2) Subscribe to Redis pub/sub channel 'run.{runId}'. 3) Forward each Redis message as SSE event (event: {type}, data: {json}). Register route in routes/api.php. WHY: Real-time progress streaming to the frontend canvas. Tests: feature test that GET /stream returns correct SSE headers, test that catchup event contains current run state, test events are formatted as valid SSE.

title: [P2-S6] Implement POST /runs/{id}/review API endpoint
description: Create POST /api/runs/{id}/review endpoint in RunController (or ReviewController). Accepts: {nodeId: string, decision: 'approve'|'reject', notes?: string}. Validates run exists and is in awaitingReview status, validates nodeId matches the awaiting node. Updates the NodeRunRecord's output_payloads with {decision, notes, reviewedAt}. The RunExecutor's review polling loop (aimodel-597) detects this change and resumes execution. WHY: Enables human-in-the-loop review at ReviewCheckpoint nodes. Tests: feature test that POST /review with valid decision updates NodeRunRecord, test that reviewing a non-awaiting run returns 422, test that invalid decision value returns 422.

title: [P2-S6] Implement cancellation flow POST /runs/{id}/cancel
description: Create POST /api/runs/{id}/cancel endpoint in RunController. Validates run is in 'running' or 'awaitingReview' status. Sets execution_runs.status = 'cancelled'. The RunExecutor's cooperative cancellation check (run.refresh() at each loop iteration in aimodel-579) detects this and marks all remaining pending/awaitingReview nodes as 'cancelled', then broadcasts RunCompleted with terminationReason='userCancelled'. WHY: Users need to stop long-running or stuck workflows. Tests: feature test that POST /cancel on running run sets status=cancelled, test that cancelling already-completed run returns 422, test that remaining nodes are marked cancelled.

title: [P2-S6] Feature tests for streaming, review, and cancel
description: Feature tests: SSE endpoint returns events in correct order (catchup then live), review flow (trigger run with reviewCheckpoint node → run pauses at awaitingReview → submit review decision → run resumes and completes), cancellation flow (trigger run → cancel mid-execution → remaining nodes are cancelled → terminal status correct).

title: [P2-S5] Implement RunExecutor main loop
description: Implement app/Domain/Execution/RunExecutor — the main execution loop. Constructor deps: ExecutionPlanner, InputResolver, NodeTemplateRegistry, RunCache, ProviderRouter, ArtifactStoreContract. Method execute(ExecutionRun): 1) plan via ExecutionPlanner, 2) set status=running + broadcast RunStarted, 3) iterate orderedNodeIds: cooperative cancellation check via run.refresh(), resolve template from registry, skip disabled nodes, resolve inputs via InputResolver, check RunCache for hit, execute template, store outputs in cache, write NodeRunRecord for each state. Catches ReviewPendingException (delegates to handleReviewCheckpoint — full implementation in aimodel-597) and Throwable (error record). Finally: deriveTerminalStatus(). Note: broadcast() calls use simple event classes; full broadcasting wiring is Step 6. Tests: unit test with mocked deps — verify node execution order follows plan, verify cache hit skips execution, verify error in one node doesn't crash run, verify disabled nodes are skipped with correct skip_reason.

title: [Phase2] Epic: Real AI Provider Adapters
description: Wire real AI provider APIs: ProviderContract interface, ProviderRouter for per-node provider selection, OpenAI and Replicate/Fal adapters, LocalArtifactStore, and an end-to-end test producing real images.

title: [P2-S7] Extend ProviderRouter with real adapter resolution
description: Extend the existing ProviderRouter (created in Step 2 with stub-only support) to resolve real adapters: openai → OpenAiAdapter, anthropic → AnthropicAdapter, replicate → ReplicateAdapter, fal → FalAdapter. Each adapter is instantiated with apiKey and model from the node's config array (per-node credentials, not .env). Tests: unit test that resolve() returns correct adapter class for each driver string, test that unknown driver throws InvalidArgumentException.

title: [P2-S7] Implement OpenAiAdapter (text generation)
description: Implement app/Domain/Providers/Adapters/OpenAiAdapter. Supports TextGeneration (POST /v1/chat/completions with system+user messages, temperature, optional json response_format), TextToImage (POST /v1/images/generations with prompt+size), TextToSpeech (POST /v1/audio/speech with input+voice+model). Uses Laravel Http client with 120s timeout. Include tests with Http::fake() recorded fixtures.

title: [P2-S7] Implement ReplicateAdapter or FalAdapter (text-to-image)
description: Create backend/app/Domain/Providers/Adapters/ReplicateAdapter.php (or FalAdapter.php) implementing ProviderContract. Supports TextToImage capability. For Replicate: POST to /v1/predictions with model version, poll GET /v1/predictions/{id} until status=succeeded, download output image URL. For Fal: POST to queue endpoint, poll for result. Accept prompt and resolution parameters from input array. Return raw image bytes. Use Laravel Http client with appropriate timeouts (initial: 30s, poll: 120s). WHY: Enables real image generation in the pipeline for ImageGeneratorTemplate. Tests: unit test with Http::fake() verifying correct API calls, test polling loop handles succeeded/failed states, test timeout on long-running predictions.

title: [P2-S7] Wire M1 templates to real providers
description: Update the 4 Milestone 1 executable templates to build proper AI prompts instead of relying on StubAdapter: ScriptWriterTemplate → builds system prompt (screenplay structure, style, duration), user prompt (from input Prompt), calls provider(TextGeneration), parses JSON response into script structure. SceneSplitterTemplate → takes script, instructs LLM to split into scenes with visual descriptions, returns sceneList. PromptRefinerTemplate → takes sceneList, generates optimized image prompts per scene, returns promptList. ImageGeneratorTemplate → iterates prompts, calls provider(TextToImage) per prompt, stores each image as artifact, returns imageFrameList/imageAssetList. Each template should handle provider errors gracefully and produce meaningful previewText. WHY: Connects the template system to real AI providers for the vertical slice pipeline. Tests: unit test each template with Http::fake() — verify correct prompt construction, verify output parsing, test error handling for provider failures.

title: [P2-S7] Implement LocalArtifactStore
description: Create backend/app/Services/LocalArtifactStore.php implementing ArtifactStoreContract. put(runId, nodeId, name, contents, mimeType): store file at artifacts/{runId}/{nodeId}/{name} on Storage::disk('local'), create Artifact model with size_bytes=strlen(contents). url(Artifact): return url('/api/artifacts/{artifact->id}'). get(Artifact): read from Storage::disk('local'). delete(Artifact): remove file + delete DB record. deleteForRun(runId): remove all artifacts for run. Also add GET /api/artifacts/{id} endpoint in ArtifactController that streams the file with correct Content-Type. WHY: Local file storage for generated images/audio/video artifacts. Tests: feature test put() stores file and creates record, get() retrieves correct bytes, delete() removes both file and record, GET /api/artifacts/{id} returns file with correct mime type.

title: [P2-S7] End-to-end test: workflow producing real images

Include: JSON request/response bodies, status codes, error format, query parameters for list endpoint. WHY: Reference for frontend integration. Acceptance: every endpoint has at least one request+response example.

WHY: Consistent error format for frontend consumption, graceful degradation. Acceptance: all error responses use structured format, provider errors include original message, SSE disconnect doesn't crash worker.

title: [P2-S8] Cache retention / garbage collection

Deletes run_cache_entries where last_accessed_at < now() - configurable TTL (default 7 days, via config/aimodel.php 'cache_ttl_days'). Optional --max-entries flag to cap total cache size. Register in app/Console/Kernel.php schedule for daily execution. WHY: Prevents unbounded cache growth in production. Acceptance: command deletes expired entries, keeps recent ones, --dry-run flag shows what would be deleted.

title: [P2-S8] Logging for debugging provider calls
description: Add structured logging for provider API calls in adapter classes. Log at info level on success: {provider, capability, model, duration_ms, token_usage?, status_code}. Log at warning level on retry. Log at error level on failure: {provider, capability, error_message, status_code}. NEVER log API keys or full prompt content. Use Laravel Log::channel('providers') with daily rotation. WHY: Debugging provider interactions without exposing sensitive data. Acceptance: running a workflow with StubAdapter produces info-level log entries, error scenarios produce error-level entries, no API keys appear in logs.

Unit tests for each validation rule.

title: [P2-S3] Implement StubAdapter for deterministic mock execution
description: Create backend/app/Domain/Providers/Adapters/StubAdapter.php implementing ProviderContract. Returns deterministic mock data for all Capability types: TextGeneration → returns structured JSON matching expected script/scene/prompt formats (e.g., {title, hook, beats[], narration, cta} for script), TextToImage → returns fake PNG bytes (valid PNG header + minimal data), TextToSpeech → returns fake audio bytes, StructuredTransform → returns mock transformed data, MediaComposition → returns mock video data. Uses SHA256-hash-based determinism: hash input to select from predefined response templates, ensuring same inputs always produce same outputs. Also register 'stub' case in ProviderRouter's resolve() method. WHY: All unit and feature tests use StubAdapter to avoid real API calls while maintaining structurally valid outputs. Tests: unit test execute() returns valid data for each Capability, test determinism (same input → identical output across calls), test output structures match what each template's execute() expects to parse.

title: [P2-S6] Implement ReviewCheckpoint polling with 1hr timeout and auto-reject
description: Implement handleReviewCheckpoint() method inside RunExecutor (extends aimodel-579). When ReviewPendingException is caught: 1) write NodeRunRecord with status=awaitingReview and input payloads, 2) update ExecutionRun status to awaitingReview, 3) broadcast NodeStatusChanged with awaitingReview status. Then enter polling loop: query NodeRunRecord every 2 seconds checking for status change (approved → continue, rejected → write error record). Timeout: 1 hour max polling → auto-reject with reason 'reviewTimeout'. Check for run cancellation during each poll iteration. On resume: extract review decision from output_payloads, continue main execution loop. WHY: ReviewCheckpoint is a unique node that pauses execution for human approval — polling loop is the simplest correct implementation. Tests: feature test that run pauses at reviewCheckpoint, POST /review resumes it, test timeout auto-rejects after configured duration, test cancellation during review stops polling.

title: [P2-S2] Create ProviderContract + ArtifactStoreContract interfaces + ProviderRouter skeleton
description: Create 3 files in backend/app/: 1) Domain/Providers/ProviderContract.php — interface with single method: execute(Capability capability, array input, array config): mixed. 2) Services/ArtifactStoreContract.php — interface with methods: put(string runId, string nodeId, string name, string contents, string mimeType): Artifact, url(Artifact): string, get(Artifact): string, delete(Artifact): void, deleteForRun(string runId): void. 3) Domain/Providers/ProviderRouter.php — class with resolve(Capability, array nodeConfig): ProviderContract. The Router skeleton should read nodeConfig['provider'] and use a match expression. Initially only the 'default' case exists (throws InvalidArgumentException). The 'stub' case is added by aimodel-596 (StubAdapter) and real adapter cases by aimodel-581 (Step 7). WHY: NodeExecutionContext (aimodel-553) and StubAdapter (aimodel-596) both depend on these interfaces existing. Without them, Step 2-3 code cannot reference provider/artifact contracts. Tests: unit test that ProviderContract can be implemented by an anonymous class, ProviderRouter throws for unknown driver, ArtifactStoreContract declares all required methods.

close_reason: Closed

title: Refactor RunExecutor to support propose/handleResponse cycle
description:
Modify RunExecutor.execute() to check needsHumanLoop() per node:

- If false: call execute() as before (unchanged behavior).
- If true: call propose($ctx), create PendingInteraction record, send message to channel (Telegram/UI), set NodeRunRecord status=awaitingHuman, save node_state, return (free the worker).

Add RunExecutor.resume($runId, $nodeId, HumanResponse):
- Load PendingInteraction and node_state from DB
- Rebuild NodeExecutionContext with saved inputs
- Call handleResponse($ctx, $response)
- If returns array (outputs): save to NodeRunRecord, status=success, continue executing remaining pipeline nodes
- If returns HumanProposal: update PendingInteraction with new state, send new message, return (loop)

Remove the old pollForReviewDecision() synchronous polling. Keep ReviewPendingException backward-compatible for HumanGateTemplate until it's migrated.
close_reason: RunExecutor refactored: needsHumanLoop check, executeHumanLoop(), resume(), savePendingInteraction(), continueAfterResume(). Removed synchronous pollForReviewDecision(). All 257 node tests pass.

Create frontend/src/shared/api/mutations.ts — mutation hooks: useCreateWorkflow() → POST /workflows, useSaveWorkflow(workflowId) → PUT /workflows/:id, useDeleteWorkflow() → DELETE /workflows/:id with query invalidation, useTriggerRun(workflowId) → POST /workflows/:id/runs, useSubmitReview(runId) → POST /runs/:id/review, useCancelRun(runId) → POST /runs/:id/cancel. All hooks use the API client and validate responses with Zod schemas. WHY: Reusable data-fetching layer used by all 3 screens. Tests: hooks return correct loading/error/data states with mocked API responses.

title: [P3-S2] Build SSE client for run streaming
description: Create frontend/src/shared/api/sse.ts — SSE client utility. Function: connectToRunStream(runId: string) → { eventSource: EventSource, cleanup: () => void }. Constructs URL from API base URL + /runs/{runId}/stream. Parses 4 event types: run.catchup (initial state sync — handles case where SSE connects after run already started, hydrates run store with current state), run.started (plannedNodeIds → store.startRun()), node.status (per-node updates with status, outputPayloads, durationMs, errorMessage, skipReason, usedCache → store.updateNodeRecord()), run.completed (terminal status + terminationReason → store.completeRun()). Auto-reconnect on EventSource error with exponential backoff (max 3 retries). Cleanup function closes EventSource and removes all listeners. WHY: Real-time execution progress from backend replaces the old mock executor. The run store's existing actions accept the same data shapes the backend SSE emits, so no store changes needed. Tests: SSE client parses each event type correctly, maps to correct store actions, handles connection errors with retry, cleanup stops reconnection.

description: Add GET /api/workflows/{id}/runs endpoint to RunController (or WorkflowRunController). Returns paginated list of ExecutionRuns for the given workflow, ordered by started_at DESC. Each run includes: id, status, trigger, targetNodeId, plannedNodeIds count, startedAt, completedAt, terminationReason, and summary stats (node success/error/skipped counts). Does NOT include full nodeRunRecords (use GET /api/runs/:id for that). Register in routes/api.php. WHY: Run history screen needs to list past runs for a workflow. Tests: feature test returns runs for correct workflow, respects pagination, returns empty array for workflow with no runs.

title: Research & build Wan prompt dictionary as controlled vocabulary for Prompt Refiner node
description: The Wan 2.6/2.7 prompt guide defines specific control tokens (light, tone, shot, camera, lens, composition terms) that the model actually responds to. Build a structured prompt dictionary/vocabulary that the Prompt Refiner node will use to convert story scripts into Wan-formatted prompts. Covers: multi-shot formula, R2V reference formula, sound formula, aesthetic control terms, and the full cinematic term dictionary. Source: wan2.6_guide/modelstudio-console.md

title: HumanGate node — universal pause/resume with external channels (Telegram, MCP, UI)
description: A generalized ReviewCheckpoint that can send data to any external channel (Telegram, MCP client, web UI), suspend the workflow, and resume when a response arrives. Configurable message templates, timeout with auto-fallback, and response parsing. This is the core primitive that enables async human-in-the-loop and AI-in-the-loop workflows.

title: TelegramTrigger + TelegramDeliver nodes
description: TelegramTrigger: starts a workflow from a Telegram message (extracts images + text as input). TelegramDeliver: sends final output (video, report) back to Telegram. Requires Telegram Bot API integration in Laravel backend with webhook endpoint.

title: Workflow execution via MCP — AI can trigger, operate, and design workflows
description: Expose the workflow engine as MCP tools so AI agents can: (1) trigger workflow runs, (2) respond to HumanGate pause points (AI-in-the-loop instead of human), (3) design/modify workflow graphs programmatically. The UI becomes the design surface, but operation can be driven by human OR AI interchangeably. MCP tools needed: workflow.run, workflow.respond, workflow.design, workflow.status.

title: Parallel Story Writer compete pattern — multi-LLM diverge node
description: A node type that fans out the same input to N parallel LLM calls (Claude, GPT, Gemini, Grok), collects all outputs, and presents them for selection (via HumanGate or AI selector). Reusable pattern for any creative step where competition improves quality.

title: ProductImageInput node — accept product images + description as workflow entry
description: Replaces userPrompt for TVC workflows. Accepts multiple product images + text description. Can be triggered from UI upload or TelegramTrigger. Outputs structured product data (images as binary/URLs + parsed description) for ProductAnalyzer.

title: ProductAnalyzer node — vision AI extracts product features from images
description: Uses vision-capable LLM (GPT-4o, Gemini) to analyze product images. Outputs: product type, colors, materials, style, unique selling points, price positioning, target audience. This feeds the StoryWriter and TrendResearcher with structured product intelligence.

title: TrendResearcher node — Grok/Gemini fetch Vietnamese TikTok trends
description: Uses social-connected LLMs (Grok backed by X, Gemini backed by Google/YouTube) to fetch current Vietnamese TikTok trends: trending formats, sounds, hashtags, cultural moments. Outputs a trend brief that feeds the StoryWriter. Runs Grok + Gemini in parallel, merges results.

title: StoryWriter node — Vietnamese GenZ story-driven TVC scripts
description: Core creative node. Takes product analysis + trend brief + model roster + optional user seed idea. Writes a human story arc (not product pitch) localized for Vietnamese GenZ. Outputs: story arc (multi-shot), cast selection from model roster, formula used, tone direction, sound direction. Works with Diverge node for multi-LLM compete pattern.

title: Diverge node — parallel multi-LLM compete pattern
description: Generic fan-out node. Sends same input to N parallel LLM providers (Claude, GPT, Gemini, Grok), collects all outputs, presents for selection via HumanGate or AI selector. Reusable for any creative step. First use case: StoryWriter compete.

title: WanR2V node — Wan 2.7 Reference-to-Video generation
description: Core video generation node using Wan R2V model via Fal API. Accepts: up to 3 character reference videos + multi-shot prompt (from PromptRefiner). Outputs: full video with voice/BGM. Supports multi-shot formula with timestamps, character tags, sound descriptions. ~/bin/zsh.75 per generation. 1080P/720P.

title: WanI2V node — Wan 2.7 Image-to-Video for product shots
description: Converts still product photos into animated video clips using Wan I2V. Used for: product reveal close-ups, unboxing animations, product showcase scenes. Prompt formula: Motion + Camera movement. Preserves subject/style/text fidelity.

title: WanImageEdit node — Wan 2.7 image editing via natural language
description: Modifies images via text commands. Use cases: put product on virtual model, change outfit, swap background, adjust style. Preserves geometric consistency. Used in fallback pipeline when R2V can't accurately render the product.

title: WanVideoEdit node — Wan 2.7 video post-processing
description: Edit generated videos via prompts. Supports: color grading, local/global editing, video reshaping, style transfer. Used to polish R2V output — fix lighting issues, adjust mood, improve cinematic quality.

title: Refactor PromptRefiner for Wan controlled vocabulary
description: Modify existing PromptRefiner node to output Wan-formatted prompts using the controlled vocabulary from wan2.6_guide. Must support: multi-shot formula with timestamps, R2V character tags, sound formula (voice/SFX/BGM), aesthetic control terms (light, tone, shot, camera, lens, composition). title: Research node frameworks for vibe-controlled AI workflow design
description:
## Context

The 8-node pipeline (test_prompts/) was tested end-to-end with Cocoon serum. Each node individually produced good output — clean brief, solid grounding, tight compliance. But the assembled video felt like a TV ad. The root cause: vibe was never explicitly controlled across the chain. The drift started at format-library-matcher (locked into ingredient-breakdown style) and every downstream node faithfully reinforced it, producing informative but low-drama output.

## Goal

Produce the knowledge base that all implementation beads under AiModel-645 build from. The output is not a narrative analysis — it is a **per-node buildable framework** specific enough that an agent can write the LLM prompt, define the input/output schema, and wire the config knobs for each node without re-running discovery.

The framework must be **agent-friendly**: a dedicated AI planner agent will read these node guides to compose workflows. The planner receives a vibe description from the user (e.g. "funny, genz, storytelling, entertainment") and outputs a workflow config — node selection + per-node knob values. That workflow config is then reusable across any product: feed Cocoon serum, coffee brand, or fashion brand through the same configured workflow and get the same vibe with different content.

This means every node framework must cleanly separate:
- **Vibe config** (set once by planner, locked across products): hook style, tension level, narrative structure, edit grammar, product visibility, humor level, CTA softness...
- **Product content** (changes per run, flows through as data): brief, claims, ingredients, constraints, visual facts...

## Scope — two types of work

**Existing nodes (research + framework extraction):** Study test_prompts/ artifacts and the pipeline plan in .cursor/plans/. For each existing node (brief-ingest, intent-outcome-selector, truth-constraint-gate, format-library-matcher, hook-angle-generator, beat-planner-shot-compiler, render-spec-compiler, edit-audio-caption-finalizer), extract a buildable framework from the experiment data.

**New vibe-control nodes (design):** For nodes that don't exist yet (workflow-designer, hook-novelty-scorer, ad-likeness-lint, native-edit-grammar), the output is a design spec — what the node should do, what it scores/rejects, and how it interacts with the vibe state. These are informed by the diagnosed failures in the existing run.

## Per-node framework output (10 sections)

1. **Purpose & pipeline position** — what the node does and where it sits in the chain.
2. **Input schema** — what structured data the node receives from its direct upstream node (not the original brief).
3. **Output schema** — what it produces for downstream consumption.
4. **Config knobs the planner can set** — named parameters that change the node's behavior. For each knob: what it controls, valid range/options, and how different settings produce different vibe outcomes. All knobs must be product-independent.
5. **Vibe impact classification** — classify as vibe-critical (format-library-matcher, hook-angle-generator, beat-planner, native-edit-grammar, ad-likeness-lint, hook-novelty-scorer) or vibe-neutral (brief-ingest, truth-constraint-gate, render-spec-compiler). Vibe-critical nodes get deeper analysis.
6. **The 4 node analysis questions**, answered with concrete test_prompts/ examples where applicable:
   - How can the node manipulate or normalize its output?
   - Should the node manipulate the output at all, or preserve its direct input more strictly?
   - What data should the node extract from raw product information and the user prompt?
   - How does this node's output impact downstream node choice, node configuration, and drift risk?
7. **Anti-patterns observed** — what went wrong in the Cocoon experiment that this node contributed to, or would have caught if it existed.
8. **Contrasting behavior examples** — for vibe-critical nodes, describe how the node should behave differently for at least two intents: soft story-sell vs. direct product-centered intro.
9. **Agent-friendly config guide** — for each config knob: plain-language description readable by an LLM planner, creative outcome (not technical behavior), example mappings (for vibe X set to Y), confirmation the knob is product-independent.
10. **Vibe state contract** — what vibe dimensions this node reads from the shared vibe state, and what it writes back. Ensures the planner can trace vibe consistency across the whole chain.

## Key architectural constraint: workflow-as-template

The planner agent creates a workflow config (JSON: node IDs + knob values) that is:
- Serializable and savable as a reusable template
- Product-agnostic: same config, different products, same vibe
- Iterable: user can ask planner to adjust ("less product-heavy", "more twist") before running

The node frameworks must support this by keeping vibe knobs strictly separate from product content.
acceptance_criteria:
- Per-node output follows the 10-section framework. An agent can start building the LLM prompt and schema for any node from this output alone.
- Existing nodes reference specific test_prompts/ artifacts (file names, JSON keys) as evidence — not vague summaries.
- New vibe-control nodes include a design spec with proposed input/output schemas, scoring dimensions, and rejection criteria.
- Vibe-critical nodes include contrasting behavior examples for at least two intents (soft story-sell, direct product-centered intro). Vibe-neutral nodes can skip this.
- The diagnosed ad-drift chain is documented: which node started the drift, how each downstream node amplified it, and where the new vibe nodes would have intervened.
- All config knobs are product-independent — they control how content is presented, not what content to use.
- Each node's framework includes an agent-friendly config guide with plain-language knob descriptions and vibe-mapping examples that an LLM planner can consume.
- Each node's framework includes a vibe state contract: what vibe dimensions it reads and writes.
- Output is structured as one section per node (not a single narrative document), so individual implementation beads can reference their section directly.
- Another agent can start the implementation beads under AiModel-645 from this research output without re-running the full discovery process.
close_reason: All research complete. 10 node frameworks written to docs/plans/. Pipeline redesigned: 12 nodes (from original 8), 2 human gates, 3 story variants (story-writer/beat-planner/mood-sequencer), hook library, casting with persona matching, prompt-enhancer for multi-provider support, workflow-designer planner agent. Key design decisions: vibe config flows from planner through all nodes, entertainment_ratio as primary anti-ad-drift knob, conversational human gates with prompt-back, provider-neutral clip specs. Deferred: ad-likeness-lint and native-edit-grammar (reopen when pipeline running).

title: Implement Telegram webhook routing for PendingInteraction
description:
Update the Telegram webhook handler to route incoming messages to the correct PendingInteraction:

1. Inline button callback: parse callback_data (already has run+node encoded) → build HumanResponse{type: pick} → dispatch ResumeJob.

2. Reply-to message: extract reply_to_message.message_id → lookup PendingInteraction by channel_message_id → build HumanResponse from text (detect pick/edit/prompt_back) → dispatch ResumeJob.

3. Bare text (no reply-to): lookup PendingInteraction by chat_id + status=waiting. If exactly 1 match → route to it. If multiple → reply asking user to reply-to specific message. If 0 → ignore.

Always use force_reply:true when sending proposals so Telegram defaults to reply-to mode.

Create ResumeJob (queued job) that calls RunExecutor.resume().

Never prune: latest recovery snapshot per workflow, runs referenced by recovery snapshots, cache rows referenced by latest successful run. Quota handling: persistWithQuotaRecovery wrapper that prunes and retries on QuotaExceededError. Cache key: node type + config hash + normalized input payload hash + workflow schema version + node template version. Methods: getReusableEntry, put, invalidate. Bounded storage with LRU eviction. Invalidation on config/topology changes. Cache hits produce success with usedCache:true, displayed in inspector. File: src/features/execution/domain/run-cache.ts, utils/payload-hashing.ts. Acceptance: Cache hit reuses previous output, cache miss triggers re-execution, invalidation works on config change, storage bounded.

title: Run toolbar
description: Build the run toolbar above the canvas per plan section 6.6. Buttons: Run Workflow, Run Selected Node, Run From Here, Run Up To Here, Cancel. Display: last run status, elapsed time, mock mode indicator. Disable node-specific actions when nothing valid selected (with explanatory tooltips). Connect to runStore for state, dispatch run commands. File: src/features/canvas/components/run-toolbar.tsx. Acceptance: All 5 buttons work correctly, disabled states with tooltips, elapsed time updates during run, status reflects run state.

title: Node execution status UI
description: Implement execution status badges on canvas nodes per plan section 11.5. Status lifecycle: pending, running (with spinner), success (green check), error (red X), skipped (grey dash), cancelled (stop icon), awaitingReview (pause icon). Badges overlay on workflow-node-card component. Update reactively from runStore node records. Show skip reason tooltip (disabled, missingRequiredInputs, upstreamFailed). Acceptance: All 7 status states render correctly, badges update in real-time during mock run, tooltips explain skip reasons.

title: Review checkpoint execution
description: Implement reviewCheckpoint special execution behavior per plan section 11.10. When reviewCheckpoint node reached during execution: auto-approve mode (default in full workflow mock) passes data through immediately, manual mode enters awaitingReview status pausing the run. UI: approve/reject buttons in inspector when node is awaitingReview. Approve re-emits input as approvedPayload (preserving schema type) + reviewDecision. Reject stops downstream execution. Run status becomes awaitingReview while paused. Acceptance: Auto-approve works in workflow runs, manual review pauses run correctly, approve/reject resume/stop execution.

title: Migrate HumanGateTemplate to propose/handleResponse pattern
description:
Refactor the existing HumanGateTemplate to use the new human-loop infrastructure instead of throwing ReviewPendingException directly.

Override needsHumanLoop() → true. Implement propose(): format input data per messageTemplate, return HumanProposal. Implement handleResponse(): on pick/approve → return outputs, on reject → throw, on prompt_back → not applicable (simple gate, no regeneration).

Remove Telegram HTTP call from execute() — the executor handles channel delivery now. Remove the _gateResponse config hack. Update HumanGateTemplateTest.
close_reason: HumanGateTemplate migrated to propose/handleResponse. needsHumanLoop=true, propose() builds HumanProposal from input+template, handleResponse() passes through. Telegram HTTP removed from node. 15 tests pass.

title: Add HumanProposal and HumanResponse value objects
description:
Create the two value objects that bridge nodes and the executor for human-loop interactions.

HumanProposal: message (string), channel (enum: telegram|ui|mcp), payload (array — structured data like story versions), state (array — serializable node state for next handleResponse call).

HumanResponse: type (enum: pick|edit|prompt_back), selectedIndex (?int), editedContent (?string), feedback (?string).

Both are readonly classes in App\Domain\Nodes namespace. Include tests.

Files: src/features/workflow/commands/*.ts (add-node.ts, connect-ports.ts, disconnect-edge.ts, insert-node-on-edge.ts, delete-selection.ts, duplicate-node.ts, update-node-config.ts, history.ts).
